# Deploy Plan — Curbside Commons static showcase (plan-first; awaiting owner sign-off)

**Status: PLAN ONLY — nothing deployed, no code changed.** Written 2026-07-08 (late session) under the standing "plan-first, free-static bias" ruling. Execution fires only on the owner's explicit deploy word naming a platform (or accepting the recommendation below).

## 1. What "done" looks like

The gated whole-site Oxblood redesign (30 prerendered pages) is reachable at a public URL as a **static artifact showcase** — a portfolio demonstration, not an operated service. Verify: every route loads (/, /report, /demo, /console, /eval, /metrics, /audit, /cost, /merchant/M001..M020), the SIMULATED banner renders and survives print, zero external requests, $0/month.

## 2. Identity constraints (binding)

- **Prototype, not a service** (owner-set 2026-06-11): the deploy is a static file drop, redeployed on demand. No uptime claims, no ops posture, no scheduled builds. Public docs must not describe it as "live infrastructure."
- Honesty invariants ride along unchanged: SIMULATED banner, footer honesty paragraph, simulated labels — the deploy changes hosting, never content.
- Deploy happens **from a committed state** — the four-slice redesign must be committed first (owner act ⓪) so the deployed artifact is SHA-traceable.

## 3. Technical shape (verified against the working tree, 2026-07-08)

- Next.js ^16.0.6, App Router; **all 30 routes prerender Static/SSG** (live `npm run build` this session); no server features in `app/` (grep: no `use server` / `next/headers` / `cookies()`).
- Execution slice = one config line (`output: "export"` in `next.config.ts`) → `next build` emits a pure-static `out/` → upload. Risk: `output: "export"` disables the default image optimizer and any future server features — acceptable; the site uses neither today. This config change is product code → standard gates (verify 947+6 floor, test:legacy 306+5, one Codex changed-files review).

## 4. Platform pick (live freshness checks, all fetched 2026-07-08)

| Option | Cost | Fit | Source (2026-07-08) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Cloudflare Pages — direct upload (RECOMMENDED)** | $0 — static asset requests "free and unlimited"; 500 deploys/mo on Free | Best fit: `wrangler pages deploy out/` is an **episodic manual push** — no git integration, no repo access granted, no standing build pipeline; matches prototype-run-on-demand. `*.pages.dev` subdomain, no domain purchase. | developers.cloudflare.com/workers/platform/pricing/ + developers.cloudflare.com/pages/ ("Available on all plans") |
| **Vercel Hobby (free alternative)** | $0 — Hobby plan free, non-commercial/personal use only (fits a portfolio prototype) | Native Next.js host; supports private repos; CLI deploy possible without git integration. Slightly heavier identity: Vercel wants an account-linked project. | vercel.com/docs/plans/hobby (doc dated 2026-06-16) |
| **GitHub Pages** | $0 but **BLOCKED while the repo is private** — Free plan serves Pages from public repos only | Becomes viable after the owner's public flip; would keep everything in one vendor. Not available today. | docs.github.com/en/get-started/learning-about-github/githubs-plans |

Enterprise-expansion path (documented, not built): the same static `out/` behind any org CDN (Cloudflare Enterprise / AWS CloudFront+S3 / Azure Static Web Apps) with CI-gated deploys.

## 5. Execution slice (fires only on the owner's word)

1. Pre-req check: redesign committed (owner act ⓪); working tree clean.
2. Add `output: "export"` to `next.config.ts`; run `npm run verify` (floor 947+6) + `test:legacy` (306+5); confirm `out/` carries all 30 pages.
3. Codex changed-files review (config diff; xhigh not warranted — one line, but ship-gating visibility → route per effort rule at review time).
4. `wrangler pages deploy out/` (or `vercel deploy --prebuilt` if the owner picks Vercel) — authenticated by the owner's own account login (owner action; agents hold no credentials — RULES §11).
5. Post-deploy smoke: fetch every route, banner present, print stylesheet intact, zero external requests (browser network log).
6. Record: decision-log row (platform + URL + date), PROJECT_STATE/HANDOFF sync, README note only if the owner extends the name/README (separate act ②).

## 6. Open owner inputs

- **The deploy word itself** + platform pick (recommendation: Cloudflare Pages direct upload).
- Whether the public URL should carry the "Curbside Commons" name only in-site (current ruling) or also in the subdomain slug (e.g. `curbside-commons.pages.dev`) — cosmetic, but it's naming, so it's yours.
- Timing relative to act ② (repo/README rename) and the public flip — independent; the deploy does not expose the repo.

 succeeded in 289ms:
import type { Metadata } from "next";
import Link from "next/link";
import { getReplaySnapshot, type ReplayMerchant } from "@/legacy/activation/lib/replay/run";
import { PLATFORM_NAME } from "@/lib/product";
import { Mark } from "@/components/data-surfaces/Mark";

export const metadata: Metadata = { title: "Audit" };

function finalState(rm: ReplayMerchant): string {
  if (rm.outreachStatus === "simulated_sent") return "Simulated sent";
  if (rm.outreachStatus === "draft_rejected") return "Rejected";
  if (rm.merchant.review_required) return "Held for review";
  return "Drafted";
}

export default function AuditPage() {
  const snap = getReplaySnapshot(PLATFORM_NAME);

  return (
    <main className="ds-data ds-wrap ds-view">
      <h1>Audit Trail</h1>
      <p className="ds-lead plain">
        <b>In plain terms:</b> every merchant&apos;s decision is recorded — what was found, what the
        gatekeeper said, how the draft scored, and what happened. No black boxes.
      </p>
      <p className="ds-runline">
        Run executed deterministically at{" "}
        <span className="ds-mono" style={{ color: "var(--ink-2)" }}>
          {snap.generatedAt}
        </span>{" "}
        (mode {snap.servedMode}). Open a merchant for its full step-by-step trail.
      </p>

      <section style={{ marginTop: "22px" }}>
        <div className="ds-tbl" style={{ marginTop: 0 }}>
          <table>
            <thead>
              <tr>
                <th scope="col">Merchant</th>
                <th scope="col">Triage</th>
                <th scope="col">Gatekeeper</th>
                <th scope="col">Eval</th>
                <th scope="col">Outcome</th>
                <th scope="col">Trail</th>
              </tr>
            </thead>
            <tbody>
              {snap.merchants.map((rm) => (
                <tr key={rm.merchant.merchant_id}>
                  <td style={{ fontWeight: 550, color: "var(--ink)" }}>{rm.merchant.merchant_name}</td>
                  <td className="ds-mono" style={{ fontSize: "12px" }}>
                    {rm.merchant.risk_level} · {rm.merchant.current_blocker_code}
                  </td>
                  <td>
                    <span className={rm.gatekeeper.status === "PASS" ? "ds-verdict ok" : "ds-verdict warn"}>
                      <Mark name={rm.gatekeeper.status === "PASS" ? "check" : "flag"} />
                      {rm.gatekeeper.status}
                    </span>
                  </td>
                  <td className="ds-mono">
                    {rm.evalScore.passed}/{rm.evalScore.total}
                  </td>
                  <td>{finalState(rm)}</td>
                  <td>
                    <Link
                      href={`/merchant/${rm.merchant.merchant_id}`}
                      className="ds-mlink"
                      style={{ fontSize: "12.5px", fontWeight: 500, color: "var(--muted)" }}
                    >
                      view ({rm.audit.length} steps)
                    </Link>
                  </td>
                </tr>
              ))}
            </tbody>
          </table>
        </div>
      </section>
    </main>
  );
}
import type { Metadata } from "next";
import Link from "next/link";
import { getReplaySnapshot, type ReplayMerchant } from "@/legacy/activation/lib/replay/run";
import { PLATFORM_NAME, HONEST_DATA_LABEL } from "@/lib/product";
import { Mark } from "@/components/data-surfaces/Mark";

export const metadata: Metadata = { title: "Console" };

const RISK_CHIP: Record<string, string> = { Low: "low", Medium: "medium", High: "high" };

function statusBadge(rm: ReplayMerchant): { text: string; cls: string } {
  if (rm.outreachStatus === "simulated_sent") return { text: "Simulated sent", cls: "sent" };
  if (rm.outreachStatus === "draft_rejected") return { text: "Rejected", cls: "rejected" };
  if (rm.merchant.review_required) return { text: "Held for review", cls: "held" };
  return { text: "Drafted", cls: "neutral" };
}

function Stat({
  label,
  value,
  sub,
  accent,
}: {
  label: string;
  value: string;
  sub?: string;
  accent?: boolean;
}) {
  return (
    <div className={accent ? "ds-stat accent" : "ds-stat"}>
      <div className="v">{value}</div>
      <div className="l">{label}</div>
      {sub ? <div className="s">{sub}</div> : null}
    </div>
  );
}

const PIPELINE: Array<{ step: string; plain: string; tech: string }> = [
  { step: "Triage", plain: "Find who's stuck, and how badly.", tech: "Deterministic risk score + level (auditable formula)." },
  { step: "Diagnose", plain: "Pin the exact blocker.", tech: "Onboarding-step → blocker/next-action map." },
  { step: "Draft", plain: "Write the right next message.", tech: "Bounded, schema-constrained LLM (REPLAY here; a real Gemini run is recorded — see Eval)." },
  { step: "Gate", plain: "Check each declared claim against the data.", tech: "Claims-gatekeeper: each declared claim traces to merchant data + forbidden-claim guardrails (undeclared prose isn't fully semantically verified — a documented boundary)." },
  { step: "Score", plain: "Measure draft quality.", tech: "Eval graders: structure · state-consistency · policy · no-leakage." },
  { step: "Approve", plain: "A human decides: hold / reject / send.", tech: "Human-in-the-loop gate; simulated send; full audit trail." },
];

export default function Console() {
  const snap = getReplaySnapshot(PLATFORM_NAME);
  const s = snap.summary;

  return (
    <main className="ds-data ds-wrap ds-view">
      <p className="ds-kicker">
        <Mark name="record" />
        {PLATFORM_NAME} · stalled-merchant activation
      </p>

      <h1>Activate stalled, long-tail merchants — responsibly.</h1>
      <p className="ds-lead plain">
        <b>In plain terms:</b> it finds which delivery-marketplace merchants are stuck getting set
        up and why, drafts the next message with every claim checked against the merchant&apos;s own
        data, and keeps a person in charge — built to be measured, audited, and adopted.
      </p>
      <p className="ds-lead tech">
        <b>Technically:</b> deterministic risk + blocker triage → bounded, schema-constrained LLM
        drafting → a claims-gatekeeper that ties every declared claim to the merchant&apos;s own data
        → an eval harness over the output → a human approval gate with an audit trail. Avoids the
        false-claim/churn failure the AI-outreach wave is hitting.
      </p>

      <div className="ds-note warn">
        <b>Honest data label:</b> {HONEST_DATA_LABEL}
      </div>

      <section className="ds-stats c6">
        <Stat label="Merchants" value={String(s.merchants)} sub="hybrid set" />
        <Stat label="Simulated sent" value={String(s.sent)} sub="eligible + clean" />
        <Stat label="Held for review" value={String(s.held)} sub="human gate" />
        <Stat label="Eval passing" value={`${s.evalPassed}/${s.evalTotal}`} sub="quality dims" />
        <Stat
          label="Gemini spend"
          value="$0.00"
          sub={`≤ $5 cap · ${snap.costLedger.liveCalls} live calls`}
          accent
        />
        <Stat label="Mode" value="REPLAY" sub="demo makes no live calls" />
      </section>

      <section>
        <h2 className="ds-h2-row">How it works &amp; why it&apos;s safe</h2>
        <div className="ds-grid g3">
          {PIPELINE.map((p) => (
            <div key={p.step} className="ds-card">
              <div className="ds-card-t">{p.step}</div>
              <p className="ds-card-p">{p.plain}</p>
              <p className="ds-card-tech">{p.tech}</p>
            </div>
          ))}
        </div>
      </section>

      <section>
        <h2 className="ds-h2-row">Activation queue</h2>
        <p className="ds-runline">
          Fictional businesses with synthetic activation state — the adapter ingests real DataSF
          public records (fictional display, real-data capability). Open one to see the full
          why-chain end to end.
        </p>
        <div className="ds-tbl">
          <table>
            <thead>
              <tr>
                <th>Merchant</th>
                <th>Category</th>
                <th>Risk</th>
                <th>Blocker</th>
                <th>Quality</th>
                <th>Status</th>
              </tr>
            </thead>
            <tbody>
              {snap.merchants.map((rm) => {
                const badge = statusBadge(rm);
                return (
                  <tr key={rm.merchant.merchant_id}>
                    <td>
                      <Link href={`/merchant/${rm.merchant.merchant_id}`} className="ds-mlink">
                        {rm.merchant.merchant_name}
                      </Link>
                    </td>
                    <td>{rm.merchant.merchant_category}</td>
                    <td>
                      <span className={`ds-chip ${RISK_CHIP[rm.merchant.risk_level]}`}>
                        {rm.merchant.risk_level} · {rm.merchant.risk_score}
                      </span>
                    </td>
                    <td className="ds-mono" style={{ fontSize: "12px" }}>
                      {rm.merchant.current_blocker_code}
                    </td>

codex
The deploy plan itself names this as full-loop/publishing-sensitive, but the implementation change is narrow: `output: "export"` only. I’m now verifying the dynamic route and exported artifact details because those are the main places static export can surprise an App Router project.

 succeeded in 0ms:
import type { CSSProperties, ReactNode } from "react";
import type { Metadata } from "next";
import Link from "next/link";
import { notFound } from "next/navigation";
import { getReplaySnapshot, getReplayMerchant } from "@/legacy/activation/lib/replay/run";
import { PLATFORM_NAME, HONEST_DATA_LABEL } from "@/lib/product";
import { TOTAL_STEPS } from "@/legacy/activation/lib/core/constants";
import { DIMENSION_SPECS } from "@/legacy/activation/lib/domain/effective-rubric";
import { Mark } from "@/components/data-surfaces/Mark";

export function generateStaticParams() {
  return getReplaySnapshot(PLATFORM_NAME).merchants.map((rm) => ({ id: rm.merchant.merchant_id }));
}

export async function generateMetadata({
  params,
}: {
  params: Promise<{ id: string }>;
}): Promise<Metadata> {
  const { id } = await params;
  const rm = getReplayMerchant(id, PLATFORM_NAME);
  return { title: rm ? rm.merchant.merchant_name : "Merchant" };
}

const GATE_CHIP: Record<string, string> = { PASS: "pass", WARN: "warn", BLOCKED: "blocked" };
const GATE_MARK: Record<string, "check" | "flag" | "alert"> = {
  PASS: "check",
  WARN: "flag",
  BLOCKED: "alert",
};
const RISK_CHIP: Record<string, string> = { Low: "low", Medium: "medium", High: "high" };

function Section({ title, plain, children }: { title: string; plain: string; children: ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <section className="ds-card flush">
      <h2>{title}</h2>
      <p className="ds-card-p" style={{ color: "var(--muted)", margin: "6px 0 12px" }}>
        {plain}
      </p>
      {children}
    </section>
  );
}

export default async function MerchantDetail({ params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }) {
  const { id } = await params;
  const rm = getReplayMerchant(id, PLATFORM_NAME);
  if (!rm) notFound();

  const { merchant: m, draft, gatekeeper: gate, judge, domainJudge, evalScore, diagnosis } = rm;
  const mRec = m as unknown as Record<string, unknown>;
  const stepsRemaining = TOTAL_STEPS - m.steps_completed;

  return (
    <main className="ds-data ds-wrap ds-narrow ds-view">
      <Link href="/" className="ds-back">
        <Mark name="arrow" />
        <span>Activation queue</span>
      </Link>

      <div className="ds-mhead">
        <div>
          <h1>{m.merchant_name}</h1>
          <p className="ds-msub">
            {m.merchant_category} · {m.merchant_id} · onboarding {m.steps_completed}/{TOTAL_STEPS}
          </p>
        </div>
        <span className={`ds-chip ${RISK_CHIP[m.risk_level]}`} style={{ fontSize: "11px" }}>
          {m.risk_level} risk · {m.risk_score}
        </span>
      </div>

      <div className="ds-stack" style={{ marginTop: "18px" }}>
        <Section
          title="1 · Triage & diagnosis"
          plain="How stuck this merchant is, and exactly what's blocking them — by an auditable rule, not a model guess."
        >
          <dl className="ds-fields">
            <Field label="Risk score" value={`${m.risk_score} (${m.risk_level})`} />
            <Field label="Blocker" value={m.current_blocker_code} />
            <Field label="Next best action" value={m.next_best_action} />
            <Field label="Days since signup" value={String(m.days_since_signup)} />
            <Field label="Last login (days ago)" value={String(m.last_login_days_ago)} />
            <Field label="Reason codes" value={m.risk_reason_codes.join(", ") || "—"} />
          </dl>
          <p className="ds-formula">
            risk = 2×{m.days_since_signup} + 3×{m.last_login_days_ago} + 10×({TOTAL_STEPS}−
            {m.steps_completed}) = {m.risk_score}
            <span className="dim">
              {"  "}# {stepsRemaining} step{stepsRemaining === 1 ? "" : "s"} remaining
            </span>
          </p>

          <div className="ds-sub">
            <div className="ds-tags">
              <span className="ds-tag role">Why they&apos;re stuck</span>
              <span className="ds-tag">{diagnosis.engagement_state.replace(/_/g, " ")}</span>
              <span className="ds-tag">{diagnosis.blocker_source.replace(/_/g, " ")}</span>
            </div>
            <p className="ds-card-p" style={{ marginTop: "10px" }}>
              {diagnosis.root_cause_hypothesis}
            </p>
            <div
              style={{
                marginTop: "10px",
                border: "1px solid var(--rule)",
                background: "var(--bg)",
                borderRadius: "8px",
                padding: "10px 12px",
              }}
            >
              <span className="ds-tag role" style={{ background: "none", border: "none", padding: 0 }}>
                Recommended play ·{" "}
              </span>
              <span style={{ fontSize: "12px", fontWeight: 600, color: "var(--ink)" }}>
                {diagnosis.play.touch.replace(/_/g, " ")}
              </span>
              <p className="ds-card-p" style={{ marginTop: "6px" }}>
                {diagnosis.play.action}
              </p>
              <p className="ds-card-tech" style={{ fontFamily: "var(--ff-sans)" }}>
                {diagnosis.play.rationale}
              </p>
            </div>
            <p className="ds-gate-note" style={{ marginTop: "8px" }}>
              {diagnosis.caveat}
            </p>
          </div>
        </Section>

        <Section
          title="2 · Drafted outreach"
          plain="A bounded, schema-constrained draft. Here it's the deterministic stub (REPLAY); a recorded real-Gemini run is on the Eval page — the safety machinery around it is identical either way."
        >
          <div className="ds-draft">
            <div className="ds-draft-sub">{draft.draft_subject}</div>
            <div className="ds-draft-body">{draft.draft_body}</div>
          </div>
          <p className="ds-card-tech" style={{ fontFamily: "var(--ff-sans)", color: "var(--graphite)" }}>
            Claims (each declared claim, verified against the merchant&apos;s data):
          </p>
          <ul className="ds-claims">
            {draft.claims.map((c, i) => (
              <li key={i}>
                <Mark name="check" style={{ width: "13px", height: "13px", color: "var(--ok)", flex: "none" }} />
                <code className="ds-code">
                  {c.field} = {String(c.value)}
                </code>
                <span style={{ color: "var(--muted)" }}>
                  → merchant.{c.field} = {String(mRec[c.field])}
                </span>
              </li>
            ))}
          </ul>
          <p className="ds-meta-line">
            mode: {rm.draftMode} · cost: ${rm.costUsd.toFixed(2)} · model: {draft.model_version}
          </p>
        </Section>

        <Section
          title="3 · Claims-gatekeeper"
          plain="A deterministic firewall: the draft cannot reach a human unless every declared claim checks out against the merchant's data and no forbidden-claim pattern is present."
        >
          <div className="ds-judge-h">
            <span className={`ds-chip ${GATE_CHIP[gate.status]}`}>
              <Mark name={GATE_MARK[gate.status]} />
              {gate.status}
            </span>
            <span className="ds-judge-count">
              {gate.approvedForHumanReview
                ? "Approved for the human gate"
                : "Auto-rejected (not shown to a human)"}
            </span>
          </div>
          <FlagList label="Guardrail flags" items={gate.guardrailFlags} emptyText="none — clean" />
          <FlagList label="Failures" items={gate.failures} emptyText="none" />
          <FlagList label="Warnings" items={gate.warnings} emptyText="none" tone="warn" />
        </Section>

        <Section
          title="4 · Faithfulness check (semantic judge)"
          plain="A second, independent check: an LLM from a DIFFERENT model family reads the finished message and verifies each factual sentence against the merchant's data row — catching an invented number, capability, or timeline the deterministic gatekeeper structurally can't see. Here it's the deterministic stub verdict (REPLAY, $0); the live cross-family judge (Groq gpt-oss-120b) is key-gated."
        >
          {judge ? (
            <>
              <div className="ds-judge-h">
                <span className={`ds-chip ${judge.verdict.any_unsupported ? "warn" : "pass"}`}>
                  <Mark name={judge.verdict.any_unsupported ? "flag" : "check"} />
                  {judge.verdict.any_unsupported ? "UNSUPPORTED CLAIM" : "ALL SUPPORTED"}
                </span>
                <span className="ds-judge-count">
                  {judge.verdict.claims.filter((c) => c.supported).length}/{judge.verdict.claims.length}{" "}
                  prose assertions backed by the data row
                </span>
              </div>
              <ul className="ds-judge-list">
                {judge.verdict.claims.map((c, i) => (
                  <li key={i}>
                    <Mark name={c.supported ? "check" : "x"} style={{ color: c.supported ? "var(--ok)" : "var(--error)" }} />
                    <span>
                      {c.text}
                      {c.supported && c.evidence_field ? (
                        <span className="ev">
                          {" "}
                          → <code className="ds-code">{c.evidence_field}</code>
                        </span>
                      ) : (
                        <span className="un"> → no supporting field (unsupported)</span>
                      )}
                    </span>
                  </li>
                ))}
              </ul>
              <p className="ds-meta-line">
                mode: {judge.mode} · model: {judge.modelId} · cost: ${judge.costUsd.toFixed(2)}
                {judge.errorClass ? ` · ${judge.errorClass}` : ""}
              </p>
            </>
          ) : (
            <p className="ds-card-p">
              Skipped — the gatekeeper blocked this draft, so it never reaches the semantic judge.
            </p>
          )}
        </Section>

        <Section
          title="5 · Domain quality check (domain judge)"
          plain="A third, independent check — on a different question than faithfulness. Not 'is every fact true?' but 'is this a GOOD activation message?' — scored against a cited rubric: matched to the merchant's real blocker · the right play for their engagement state · no over-promising. It's advisory and recall-favoring: the verdict is surfaced for the reviewer and recorded in the audit trail, but it never changes the send — eligibility and the human approval gate stay deterministic (a low-risk draft can still be simulated-sent even when flagged). Here BOTH the draft and this verdict are deterministic $0 stubs (REPLAY) — a minimal stub nudge often trips the engagement-fit check, which is the tertiary control doing its job, not the product grading its real output down; the live cross-family judge (Groq gpt-oss-120b) and the real drafter are separate and key-gated."
        >
          {domainJudge ? (
            <>
              <div className="ds-judge-h">
                <span className={`ds-chip ${domainJudge.verdict.domain_defective ? "warn" : "pass"}`}>
                  <Mark name={domainJudge.verdict.domain_defective ? "flag" : "check"} />
                  {domainJudge.verdict.domain_defective ? "FLAGGED FOR REVIEW" : "GOOD PRACTICE"}
                </span>
                <span className="ds-judge-count">
                  {domainJudge.verdict.dimensions.filter((d) => d.pass).length}/
                  {domainJudge.verdict.dimensions.length} domain-quality dimensions passed
                </span>
              </div>
              <ul className="ds-judge-list">
                {domainJudge.verdict.dimensions.map((d, i) => (
                  <li key={i}>
                    <Mark name={d.pass ? "check" : "flag"} style={{ color: d.pass ? "var(--ok)" : "var(--warn)" }} />
                    <span>
                      <span style={{ fontWeight: 600, color: "var(--ink)" }}>
                        {DIMENSION_SPECS[d.dimension].title}
                      </span>
                      <span className="ev"> — {d.rationale}</span>
                    </span>
                  </li>
                ))}
              </ul>
              <p className="ds-meta-line">
                mode: {domainJudge.mode} · model: {domainJudge.modelId} · cost: $
                {domainJudge.costUsd.toFixed(2)}
                {domainJudge.errorClass ? ` · ${domainJudge.errorClass}` : ""} · advisory — does not
                change the send decision
              </p>

out/cost.html:1:<!DOCTYPE html><html lang="en" data-scroll-behavior="smooth" class="geist_f880305f-module__AJLRcW__variable geist_mono_42eec60-module__Gb1_vG__variable jetbrains_mono_6104e6b7-module__s6uLTa__variable"><head><meta charSet="utf-8"/><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1"/><link rel="preload" href="/_next/static/media/70bc3e132a0a741e-s.p.3t6q91iet4nsy.woff2" as="font" crossorigin="" type="font/woff2"/><link rel="preload" href="/_next/static/media/797e433ab948586e-s.p.0r6juujl39pe6.woff2" as="font" crossorigin="" type="font/woff2"/><link rel="preload" href="/_next/static/media/caa3a2e1cccd8315-s.p.0wgildi0cnwt9.woff2" as="font" crossorigin="" type="font/woff2"/><link rel="stylesheet" href="/_next/static/chunks/1tc2hl3z9iry_.css" data-precedence="next"/><link rel="preload" as="script" fetchPriority="low" href="/_next/static/chunks/10qk6v6416kh8.js"/><script src="/_next/static/chunks/27jktro2p5rq9.js" async=""></script><script src="/_next/static/chunks/2nykiepra7i1k.js" async=""></script><script src="/_next/static/chunks/273tk-n0d__2s.js" async=""></script><script src="/_next/static/chunks/turbopack-1d2m_hmbgvnw2.js" async=""></script><script src="/_next/static/chunks/0cgk26qnkn56x.js" async=""></script><script src="/_next/static/chunks/14mrh2-p_w84d.js" async=""></script><meta name="next-size-adjust" content=""/><title>Cost · Curbside Commons</title><meta name="description" content="The truth layer for agentic commerce — a deterministic verifier of platform/AI-agent serving copies vs the merchant system-of-record, UCP conformance, and NYC fee-cap audit. Simulated corpus; company-agnostic prototype."/><script src="/_next/static/chunks/0cz1d0mv5g_q7.js" noModule=""></script></head><body><div hidden=""><!--$--><!--/$--></div><a href="#main-content" class="ds-skip">Skip to main content</a><nav aria-label="Primary" class="site-nav"><div class="site-nav-in"><a class="site-brand" href="/"><svg class="site-brand-mark" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M10 2.2l6.4 2.4v4.2c0 4.2-2.7 6.7-6.4 8.4-3.7-1.7-6.4-4.2-6.4-8.4V4.6z"></path><path d="M6.8 10.2l2.2 2.2 4.4-4.6"></path></svg>Curbside Commons</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/console">Console</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/report">Report</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/demo">Demo</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/eval">Eval / Quality</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/metrics">Metrics</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/audit">Audit</a><a aria-current="page" class="site-navlink" href="/cost">Cost</a><span class="site-status"><span class="site-status-pip" aria-hidden="true"></span>Prototype · REPLAY · $0.00</span></div></nav><div id="main-content" tabindex="-1"><main class="ds-data ds-wrap ds-narrow ds-view"><h1>Cost ledger</h1><p class="ds-lead plain"><b>In plain terms:</b>the live-drafting path is budget-guarded, so a run can&#x27;t quietly exceed the cap. Spend is computed from real reported tokens against a pinned price list, and a fail-closed hard stop blocks any call that would cross it.</p><section class="ds-stats c4"><div class="ds-stat accent"><div class="v">$<!-- -->0.00</div><div class="l">spent this run</div></div><div class="ds-stat"><div class="v">$<!-- -->5.00</div><div class="l">hard cap (fail-closed)</div></div><div class="ds-stat"><div class="v">0</div><div class="l">live calls</div></div><div class="ds-stat"><div class="v">REPLAY</div><div class="l">serve mode</div></div></section><p class="ds-note">Deterministic stub drafting — no live Gemini calls, $0.00 spend. A real Gemini run is recorded (see Eval); the public demo stays REPLAY-only.</p><section><h2 class="ds-h2-row">How the cap holds</h2><ul style="margin:10px 0 0;padding-left:18px;font-size:13px;line-height:1.8;color:var(--ink-2)"><li>Cost = real API-reported tokens × a pinned, versioned price table (not an estimate).</li><li>Before every live call, a fail-closed guard blocks it if spent + next-estimate would exceed the cap.</li><li>A batch threads cumulative spend, so the cap holds across the whole run — not just per call.</li><li>An unknown model id fails loud (never silently prices at $0); a billed-then-failed call still records its cost.</li><li>The price table was pinned + verified against official Gemini pricing for the recorded run; it must be re-checked before any future live run (never trusted from memory).</li></ul></section><section><h2 class="ds-h2-row">Pinned price table<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:400;color:var(--muted);font-family:var(--ff-sans);font-size:14px">(<!-- -->2026-06-18<!-- -->)</span></h2><div class="ds-tbl"><table><thead><tr><th scope="col">Model</th><th scope="col">Input $/1M</th><th scope="col">Output $/1M</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td class="ds-mono" style="font-size:12.5px">gemini-2.5-flash</td><td class="ds-mono">$<!-- -->0.3</td><td class="ds-mono">$<!-- -->2.5</td></tr><tr><td class="ds-mono" style="font-size:12.5px">gemini-2.5-flash-lite</td><td class="ds-mono">$<!-- -->0.1</td><td class="ds-mono">$<!-- -->0.4</td></tr><tr><td class="ds-mono" style="font-size:12.5px">gemini-2.5-pro</td><td class="ds-mono">$<!-- -->1.25</td><td class="ds-mono">$<!-- -->10</td></tr></tbody></table></div></section></main><!--$--><!--/$--></div><footer class="site-footer"><div class="site-footer-in"><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Demo / portfolio prototype.</span> <!-- -->REPLAY over <span style="font-weight:600">fictional display names</span> +<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">synthetic activation state</span>(the adapter ingests real public DataSF records; the demo shows invented ones) — not production logs, real sends, real marketplace access, or real-impact data. The “real Gemini” output shown is a<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">recorded static fixture</span> (reproduce it locally with your own key). Human-led, AI-assisted, professionally reviewed.<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to</span> <!-- -->DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, DataSF, or any named business.</div></footer><script src="/_next/static/chunks/10qk6v6416kh8.js" id="_R_" Simulated corpus; company-agnostic prototype."/><script src="/_next/static/chunks/0cz1d0mv5g_q7.js" noModule=""></script></head><body><div hidden=""><!--$--><!--/$--></div><a href="#main-content" class="ds-skip">Skip to main content</a><nav aria-label="Primary" class="site-nav"><div class="site-nav-in"><a class="site-brand" href="/"><svg class="site-brand-mark" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M10 2.2l6.4 2.4v4.2c0 4.2-2.7 6.7-6.4 8.4-3.7-1.7-6.4-4.2-6.4-8.4V4.6z"></path><path d="M6.8 10.2l2.2 2.2 4.4-4.6"></path></svg>Curbside Commons</a><a aria-current="page" class="site-navlink" href="/console">Console</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/report">Report</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/demo">Demo</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/eval">Eval / Quality</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/metrics">Metrics</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/audit">Audit</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/cost">Cost</a><span class="site-status"><span class="site-status-pip" aria-hidden="true"></span>Prototype · REPLAY · $0.00</span></div></nav><div id="main-content" tabindex="-1"><main class="ds-data ds-wrap ds-view"><p class="ds-kicker"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4.5 6h11 M4.5 10h11 M4.5 14h7"></path></svg>Curbside Commons<!-- --> · stalled-merchant activation</p><h1>Activate stalled, long-tail merchants — responsibly.</h1><p class="ds-lead plain"><b>In plain terms:</b>it finds which delivery-marketplace merchants are stuck getting set up and why, drafts the next message with every claim checked against the merchant&#x27;s own data, and keeps a person in charge — built to be measured, audited, and adopted.</p><p class="ds-lead tech"><b>Technically:</b>deterministic risk + blocker triage → bounded, schema-constrained LLM drafting → a claims-gatekeeper that ties every declared claim to the merchant&#x27;s own data → an eval harness over the output → a human approval gate with an audit trail. Avoids the false-claim/churn failure the AI-outreach wave is hitting.</p><div class="ds-note warn"><b>Honest data label:</b> <!-- -->The merchant names shown are FICTIONAL (no real businesses — so synthetic risk states are never attached to real people). The product&#x27;s adapter ingests real DataSF public-record names (PDDL 1.0, public domain — name + category only, PII-scrubbed; see lib/ingest/sf-adapter.ts). Activation state is synthetic and illustrative. No real merchant relationship or account.</div><section class="ds-stats c6"><div class="ds-stat"><div class="v">20</div><div class="l">Merchants</div><div class="s">hybrid set</div></div><div class="ds-stat"><div class="v">6</div><div class="l">Simulated sent</div><div class="s">eligible + clean</div></div><div class="ds-stat"><div class="v">14</div><div class="l">Held for review</div><div class="s">human gate</div></div><div class="ds-stat"><div class="v">20/20</div><div class="l">Eval passing</div><div class="s">quality dims</div></div><div class="ds-stat accent"><div class="v">$0.00</div><div class="l">Gemini spend</div><div class="s">≤ $5 cap · 0 live calls</div></div><div class="ds-stat"><div class="v">REPLAY</div><div class="l">Mode</div><div class="s">demo makes no live calls</div></div></section><section><h2 class="ds-h2-row">How it works &amp; why it&#x27;s safe</h2><div class="ds-grid g3"><div class="ds-card"><div class="ds-card-t">Triage</div><p class="ds-card-p">Find who&#x27;s stuck, and how badly.</p><p class="ds-card-tech">Deterministic risk score + level (auditable formula).</p></div><div class="ds-card"><div class="ds-card-t">Diagnose</div><p class="ds-card-p">Pin the exact blocker.</p><p class="ds-card-tech">Onboarding-step → blocker/next-action map.</p></div><div class="ds-card"><div class="ds-card-t">Draft</div><p class="ds-card-p">Write the right next message.</p><p class="ds-card-tech">Bounded, schema-constrained LLM (REPLAY here; a real Gemini run is recorded — see Eval).</p></div><div class="ds-card"><div class="ds-card-t">Gate</div><p class="ds-card-p">Check each declared claim against the data.</p><p class="ds-card-tech">Claims-gatekeeper: each declared claim traces to merchant data + forbidden-claim guardrails (undeclared prose isn&#x27;t fully semantically verified — a documented boundary).</p></div><div class="ds-card"><div class="ds-card-t">Score</div><p class="ds-card-p">Measure draft quality.</p><p class="ds-card-tech">Eval graders: structure · state-consistency · policy · no-leakage.</p></div><div class="ds-card"><div class="ds-card-t">Approve</div><p class="ds-card-p">A human decides: hold / reject / send.</p><p class="ds-card-tech">Human-in-the-loop gate; simulated send; full audit trail.</p></div></div></section><section><h2 class="ds-h2-row">Activation queue</h2><p class="ds-runline">Fictional businesses with synthetic activation state — the adapter ingests real DataSF public records (fictional display, real-data capability). tabindex="-1"><main class="ds-data ds-wrap ds-view"><h1>Workflow metrics (simulated)</h1><p class="ds-lead plain"><b>In plain terms:</b>what the demo routes and tracks for an activation team — how many stalled merchants get a claim-checked nudge, how many are held for a human, and what&#x27;s blocking them.</p><div class="ds-note warn">Figures are <b>simulated</b> over the hybrid demo set (fictional names, synthetic activation state) — illustrative of the workflow, <b>not activation, revenue, or reactivation outcomes</b>.</div><section class="ds-stats c4"><div class="ds-stat"><div class="v">20</div><div class="l">Stalled merchants</div></div><div class="ds-stat"><div class="v">6</div><div class="l">Simulated sent</div></div><div class="ds-stat"><div class="v">14</div><div class="ds-card flush"><h2>Blocker mix</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin-bottom:14px">Where merchants are stuck (the work to do).</p><div class="ds-bar-row"><div A simulated prototype — not affiliated with any marketplace."/><script src="/_next/static/chunks/0cz1d0mv5g_q7.js" noModule=""></script></head><body><div hidden=""><!--$--><!--/$--></div><a href="#main-content" class="ds-skip">Skip to main content</a><nav aria-label="Primary" class="site-nav"><div class="site-nav-in"><a aria-current="page" class="site-brand" href="/"><svg class="site-brand-mark" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M10 2.2l6.4 2.4v4.2c0 4.2-2.7 6.7-6.4 8.4-3.7-1.7-6.4-4.2-6.4-8.4V4.6z"></path><path d="M6.8 10.2l2.2 2.2 4.4-4.6"></path></svg>Curbside Commons</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/console">Console</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/report">Report</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/demo">Demo</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/eval">Eval / Quality</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/metrics">Metrics</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/audit">Audit</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/cost">Cost</a><span class="site-status"><span class="site-status-pip" aria-hidden="true"></span>Prototype · REPLAY · $0.00</span></div></nav><div id="main-content" tabindex="-1"><main class="lp-main"><section class="ds-wrap lp-hero" aria-labelledby="hero-h1"><div class="lp-hero-grid"><div class="lp-hero-copy"><p class="lp-eyebrow"><svg class="lp-ic" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M10 2.2l6.4 2.4v4.2c0 4.2-2.7 6.7-6.4 8.4-3.7-1.7-6.4-4.2-6.4-8.4V4.6z"></path><path d="M6.8 10.2l2.2 2.2 4.4-4.6"></path></svg>Merchant activation · review &amp; approval</p><h1 id="hero-h1">AI writes your merchant outreach — and nothing reaches a merchant until every claim is checked against <span class="lp-mark">their own data</span>.</h1><p class="lp-lede">Each message is checked against <em>that merchant’s own record</em> <!-- -->before it can send. A confident-sounding claim the data can’t back is held for a person — never sent on its own.</p><div class="lp-cta-row"><a class="lp-btn primary" href="/console">See it run on the console <svg class="lp-ic" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M3.8 10h11M10.6 5.8l4.2 4.2-4.2 4.2"></path></svg></a><a class="lp-btn ghost" href="#how">How the checking works</a></div></div><aside class="lp-hero-aside" aria-label="A recorded outreach draft, checked and held for approval"><div class="ds-panel ds-panel-strong cp cp-settled" aria-label="A recorded draft, checked line by line against this merchant&#x27;s record"><div class="cp-cap"><span>outreach draft · checked against the record</span><span class="cp-cap-right"><span class="cp-tick" aria-hidden="true"></span>held for review</span></div><div class="cp-record" aria-label="This merchant&#x27;s record"><span class="cp-rlbl">this merchant’s record · the only thing the message is checked against</span><span class="cp-rf"><span class="cp-rk">category:</span> <span class="cp-rv">Restaurant</span></span><span class="cp-rf"><span class="cp-rk">setup:</span> <span class="cp-rv ds-num">2 of 5 steps</span></span><span class="cp-rf"><span class="cp-rk">blocker:</span> <span class="cp-rv">photos needed</span></span><span class="cp-rf absent"><span class="cp-rk">go-live date:</span> <span class="cp-rv">none on file</span></span></div><div class="cp-draft"><div class="cp-claim pass"><div class="cp-ctext">“You’ve completed 2 of 5 setup steps.”</div><div class="cp-verdict"><span class="cp-vcell pass"><svg class="cp-glyph" viewBox="0 0 15 15" fill="none" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M3 8l3 3 6-7" stroke="var(--ink)" stroke-width="1.9" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"></path></svg>matches the data</span><span class="ds-mono cp-cite">→ setup: 2 of 5 steps</span></div></div><div class="cp-claim pass"><div class="cp-ctext">“Add photos to finish your listing.”</div><div class="cp-verdict"><span class="cp-vcell pass"><svg class="cp-glyph" viewBox="0 0 15 15" fill="none" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M3 8l3 3 6-7" stroke="var(--ink)" stroke-width="1.9" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"></path></svg>matches the data</span><span class="ds-mono cp-cite">→ blocker: photos needed</span></div></div><div class="cp-claim fail"><div class="cp-ctext">“You’ll be live by Friday.”</div><div class="cp-verdict"><span class="cp-vcell fail"><svg class="cp-glyph" viewBox="0 0 15 15" fill="none" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 4l7 7M11 4l-7 7" stroke="var(--signal-text)" stroke-width="1.9" stroke-linecap="round"></path></svg>not in the data</span><span class="ds-mono cp-cite">no go-live date on this record</span></div></div><div class="cp-held"><span class="cp-held-lbl">Held for a person to approve.</span><span class="ds-mono">1 claim the data doesn’t back · not sent, not rejected — it’s your call.</span></div><div class="cp-decide" aria-label="Reviewer actions"><span class="cp-dnote ds-mono">what you do next · recorded to the audit trail</span><button class="ds-btn hold" type="button" disabled="" aria-disabled="true">Keep held</button><button class="ds-btn" type="button" disabled="" aria-disabled="true">Edit the claim</button><button class="ds-btn primary" type="button" disabled="" aria-disabled="true">Approve &amp; send</button></div></div></div></aside></div></section><section class="ds-wrap ds-section" id="checks" aria-labelledby="checks-h2"><div class="ds-reveal in"><p class="lp-eyebrow"><svg class="lp-ic" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M10 2.2l6.4 2.4v4.2c0 4.2-2.7 6.7-6.4 8.4-3.7-1.7-6.4-4.2-6.4-8.4V4.6z"></path><path d="M6.8 10.2l2.2 2.2 4.4-4.6"></path></svg> How a message earns the right to send</p><h2 id="checks-h2" class="lp-h2">Three checks stand between the AI and a merchant.</h2></div><div class="ds-stagger in"><div class="lp-grid3"><div class="lp-panel"><svg class="lp-si" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M3.8 10.4l3.6 3.6L16.2 5.6"></path></svg><h3 class="lp-h3">An exact, automatic check</h3><p class="lp-ptext">Every fact the message states is matched, exactly, against the merchant’s record. No match, no pass.</p></div><div class="lp-panel"><svg class="lp-si" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M2.5 10S5.5 4.8 10 4.8 17.5 10 17.5 10 14.5 15.2 10 15.2 2.5 10 2.5 10z"></path><circle cx="10" cy="10" r="2.4"></circle></svg><h3 class="lp-h3">An independent second reviewer</h3><p class="lp-ptext">A separate AI reviewer re-reads the message for anything the record can’t back. It doesn’t grade its own work.</p></div><div class="lp-panel"><svg class="lp-si" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><circle cx="10" cy="6.6" r="3"></circle><path d="M4.2 16.8c0-3.2 2.6-5.4 5.8-5.4s5.8 2.2 5.8 5.4"></path></svg><h3 class="lp-h3">A person signs off</h3><p class="lp-ptext">Anything in question is held for your team. Approve, edit, or hold. Nothing sends itself.</p></div></div></div><div class="ds-reveal in"><p class="lp-foot">And we don’t ask you to take it on faith: we measure<!-- --> <em>how often the AI reviewer agrees with human reviewers</em>, and tune it to err toward holding rather than letting something slip through.</p></div></section><section class="ds-wrap ds-section" id="gap" aria-labelledby="gap-h2"><div class="ds-reveal in"><p class="lp-eyebrow"><svg class="lp-ic" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M10 2.6l7.6 13.4H2.4z"></path><path d="M10 8v3.6"></path><circle cx="10" cy="14.2" r=".5"></circle></svg>Why an ordinary safety check isn’t enough</p><h2 id="gap-h2" class="lp-h2">An AI hallucination sounds just as confident as the truth.</h2><p class="lp-lede lp-lede-wide">When AI states something that isn’t true for a merchant, the industry calls it a<!-- --> <em>hallucination</em>— a fabrication that reads exactly as confident as a fact. Most AI safety checks read the <em>tone</em> <!-- -->of a message: is it rude, off-policy, leaking personal data? They don’t check whether what it said is<!-- --> <em>true for this merchant</em>. So “you’ll be live by Friday,” written for an account with no go-live date, sails straight through.</p></div></section><section class="ds-wrap ds-section" id="how" aria-labelledby="how-h2"><div class="ds-reveal in"><p class="lp-eyebrow"><svg class="lp-ic" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M3 4h14l-5.2 6.2v5.4l-3.6-1.9v-3.5z"></path></svg> How it works</p><h2 id="how-h2" class="lp-h2">The AI is checked, not trusted.</h2><p class="lp-lede lp-lede-wide">Five stages. Cheap, exact checks run before the slower ones; an independent reviewer runs near the end; a person always has the last word.</p></div><div class="ds-stagger in"><div class="lp-flow" role="list" aria-label="The five-stage checking flow"><div class="lp-step" role="listitem"><svg class="lp-si" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><rect x="3.2" y="2.5" width="13.6" height="15" rx="1.6"></rect><path d="M6.2 6.2h7.6M6.2 9.6h7.6M6.2 13h4.6"></path></svg><span class="lp-sk">Your data</span><h3 class="lp-h4">The record</h3><p class="lp-ptext">The merchant’s own record is the only thing a message is allowed to be true against.</p></div><div class="lp-step" role="listitem"><svg class="lp-si" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M13.2 3.4l3.4 3.4L7.4 16H4v-3.4z"></path><path d="M11.6 5l3.4 3.4"></path></svg><span class="lp-sk">Draft</span><h3 class="lp-h4">AI drafts from it</h3><p class="lp-ptext">The AI writes the outreach from that record — not the open web, not typed-in text.</p></div><div class="lp-step gate" role="listitem"><svg class="lp-si" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M3 4h14l-5.2 6.2v5.4l-3.6-1.9v-3.5z"></path></svg><span class="lp-sk">Gate · check</span><h3 class="lp-h4">Exact automatic check</h3><p class="lp-ptext">Every fact must match a field in the record, exactly. Anything that doesn’t is flagged.</p></div><div class="lp-step gate" role="listitem"><svg class="lp-si" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M2.5 10S5.5 4.8 10 4.8 17.5 10 17.5 10 14.5 15.2 10 15.2 2.5 10 2.5 10z"></path><circle cx="10" cy="10" r="2.4"></circle></svg><span class="lp-sk">Judge · check</span><h3 class="lp-h4">Independent reviewer</h3><p class="lp-ptext">A separate AI reviewer flags anything the data can’t back — including facts slipped in casually.</p></div><div class="lp-step" role="listitem"><svg class="lp-si" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><circle cx="10" cy="6.6" r="3"></circle><path d="M4.2 16.8c0-3.2 2.6-5.4 5.8-5.4s5.8 2.2 5.8 5.4"></path></svg><span class="lp-sk">Approve</span><h3 class="lp-h4">A person approves</h3><p class="lp-ptext">Your reviewer approves, edits, or holds. Nothing sends itself; every decision lands on the trail.</p></div></div></div></section><section class="ds-wrap ds-section" id="vs" aria-labelledby="vs-h2"><div class="ds-reveal in"><p class="lp-eyebrow"><svg class="lp-ic" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M10 3v13M5 16h10M6.5 6l-3 5a2.5 2.5 0 005 0zM13.5 6l-3 5a2.5 2.5 0 005 0z"></path><path d="M4 6h12"></path></svg> What makes it different</p><h2 id="vs-h2" class="lp-h2">A safety filter checks the message. We check the facts.</h2></div><div class="ds-reveal in"><div class="lp-grid2"><div class="lp-panel"><div class="lp-role">an ordinary AI safety filter (“guardrail”)</div><h3 class="lp-h3">“Is this message appropriate?”</h3><p class="lp-ptext">Reads the message on its own.</p></div><div class="lp-panel ours"><div class="lp-role">Curbside Commons</div><h3 class="lp-h3">“Is this message true for this merchant?”</h3><p class="lp-ptext">Every claim checked against the data row; an exact check, then an independent reviewer; evidence on every line, held for a person. Reads the message<!-- --> <em>against the record</em>.</p></div></div></div></section><section class="ds-wrap ds-section" id="reviewer" aria-labelledby="rev-h2"><div class="ds-reveal in"><p class="lp-eyebrow"><svg class="lp-ic" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M6 8.5l3.4 3.4M4 4l1.2 3M16 4l-3.6 9.2L9 15z"></path></svg> The obvious question</p><h2 id="rev-h2" class="lp-h2">“How do you know the AI reviewer is right?”</h2><p class="lp-lede lp-lede-wide">Fair question — so we don’t ask you to assume it.<!-- --> <em>We check the reviewer against people.</em></p></div><div class="ds-stagger in"><div class="lp-grid3"><div class="lp-panel"><div class="lp-qh">We measure it against human judgment.</div><p class="lp-ptext">A calibration run has cleared its pre-registered bar on a held-out set · figures stay unpublished until a larger validation run confirms them.</p></div><div class="lp-panel"><div class="lp-qh">It’s tuned to hold, not to over-block.</div><p class="lp-ptext">A false hold costs a glance; a shipped falsehood costs trust.</p></div><div class="lp-panel"><div class="lp-qh">The exact check underneath is locked.</div><p class="lp-ptext">The deterministic comparison is fixed and auditable — it can’t drift.</p></div></div></div></section><section class="ds-wrap ds-section" id="run" aria-labelledby="run-h2"><div class="ds-reveal in"><p class="lp-eyebrow"><svg class="lp-ic" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M3.8 10h11M10.6 5.8l4.2 4.2-4.2 4.2"></path></svg> See it run</p><h2 id="run-h2" class="lp-h2">Watch one draft get checked, line by line.</h2><div class="lp-cta-row lp-cta-close"><a class="lp-btn primary" href="/console">See it run on the console <svg class="lp-ic" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M3.8 10h11M10.6 5.8l4.2 4.2-4.2 4.2"></path></svg></a><span class="lp-cta-note">a recorded, replayable run — not a sign-up.</span></div><p class="lp-note">A simulated prototype on de-identified, public open data. Merchant and reviewer names are fictional. Not affiliated with DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, or any marketplace. The walkthrough is a recorded, replayable run — labeled, not live — and accuracy figures are held until a larger validation run confirms them. Human-led, AI-assisted, professionally reviewed.</p><p class="ds-mono lp-strip">SIMULATED · FICTIONAL NAMES · NO REAL MERCHANT DATA · NOT AFFILIATED WITH ANY MARKETPLACE · REPLAY / RECORDED · FIGURES PENDING VALIDATION · HUMAN-LED, AI-ASSISTED, PROFESSIONALLY REVIEWED</p></div></section></main><!--$--><!--/$--></div><footer class="site-footer"><div class="site-footer-in"><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Demo / portfolio prototype.</span> <!-- -->REPLAY over <span style="font-weight:600">fictional display names</span> +<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">synthetic activation state</span>(the adapter ingests real public DataSF records; the demo shows invented ones) — not production logs, real sends, real marketplace access, or real-impact data. The “real Gemini” output shown is a<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">recorded static fixture</span> (reproduce it locally with your own key). Human-led, AI-assisted, professionally reviewed.<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to</span> <!-- -->DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, DataSF, or any named business.</div></footer><script src="/_next/static/chunks/10qk6v6416kh8.js" id="_R_" How a message earns the right to send\"]}],[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"id\":\"checks-h2\",\"className\":\"lp-h2\",\"children\":\"Three checks stand between the AI and a Simulated test data; deterministic; zero AI calls."/><script src="/_next/static/chunks/0cz1d0mv5g_q7.js" noModule=""></script></head><body><div hidden=""><!--$--><!--/$--></div><a href="#main-content" class="ds-skip">Skip to main content</a><nav aria-label="Primary" class="site-nav"><div class="site-nav-in"><a class="site-brand" href="/"><svg class="site-brand-mark" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M10 2.2l6.4 2.4v4.2c0 4.2-2.7 6.7-6.4 8.4-3.7-1.7-6.4-4.2-6.4-8.4V4.6z"></path><path d="M6.8 10.2l2.2 2.2 4.4-4.6"></path></svg>Curbside Commons</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/console">Console</a><a aria-current="page" class="site-navlink" href="/report">Report</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/demo">Demo</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/eval">Eval / Quality</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/metrics">Metrics</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/audit">Audit</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/cost">Cost</a><span class="site-status"><span class="site-status-pip" aria-hidden="true"></span>Prototype · REPLAY · $0.00</span></div></nav><div id="main-content" tabindex="-1"><main class="rpt-wrap" id="report"><div class="rpt-sim" role="note"><span class="rpt-sim-tag">SIMULATED</span><span class="rpt-sim-text">Synthetic test data — an invented restaurant, invented menu, invented prices. Not real DoorDash / Square / Uber Eats / Grubhub data, access, or business impact. The verification rules and the pinned data-format standard are real; the restaurant, its menu, and its records are invented.</span></div><header class="rpt-head"><p class="rpt-eyebrow">Verifier report · listings truth check</p><h1 class="rpt-title">What the copy says vs. what the restaurant’s records say</h1><p class="rpt-intro">A serving copy of a menu (what an AI shopping assistant would read) checked, line by line, against the restaurant’s own system-of-record. Below is every difference the checker caught — each in plain words first, then the exact receipts. Deterministic and $0, with no AI calls in this verifier runtime: the same input always gives this same report.</p></header><div class="rpt-toolbar" role="tablist" aria-label="serving surface"><button role="tab" type="button" aria-selected="true" class="rpt-tab active">ACP static feed</button><button role="tab" type="button" aria-selected="false" class="rpt-tab ">UCP catalog response</button></div><section class="rpt-sec" aria-labelledby="rpt-rail-verdict"><h2 id="rpt-rail-verdict" class="rpt-rail">Verdict</h2><div class="rpt-bodycol"><div class="rpt-verdict fail"><span class="rpt-verdict-flag">FAIL</span><span class="rpt-verdict-count">16 findings</span><span class="rpt-verdict-tally">11<!-- --> error · <!-- -->5<!-- --> warn · <!-- -->0<!-- --> info</span></div></div></section><section class="rpt-sec" aria-labelledby="rpt-rail-meta"><h2 id="rpt-rail-meta" class="rpt-rail">Meta</h2><div class="rpt-bodycol"><dl class="rpt-meta"><div class="rpt-mrow"><dt>surface</dt><dd>ACP static feed<!-- --> <span class="rpt-rc-sub">(<!-- -->OpenAI/Stripe product-feed shape<!-- -->)</span></dd></div><div class="rpt-mrow"><dt>spec version</dt><dd class="rpt-mono">taxonomy-v1+acp-extract-2026-07-02+ucp-pin-2026-04-08</dd></div><div class="rpt-mrow"><dt>matching mode</dt><dd><span class="rpt-mono">synthetic-controlled</span><span class="rpt-rc-sub">synthetic-controlled — the copy and the catalog share item IDs, so matching is exact (no entity resolution)</span></dd></div><div class="rpt-mrow"><dt>data</dt><dd><span class="rpt-mono">simulated: <!-- -->true</span></dd></div></dl></div></section><section class="rpt-sec" aria-labelledby="rpt-rail-findings"><h2 id="rpt-rail-findings" hidden in the merchant&#x27;s own catalog but is being served to customers.</p><span class="rpt-sev error">error</span></div><dl class="rpt-receipts" aria-label="evidence"><div class="rpt-rc"><dt>claim</dt><dd><span class="rpt-mono">item-002-v2#existence</span><span class="rpt-mono-sub">acp-feed<!-- --> · <!-- -->existence<!-- --> = <!-- -->true</span></dd></div><div class="rpt-rc"><dt>reference row</dt><dd class="rpt-mono">item-002-v2</dd></div><div class="rpt-rc"><dt>rule / spec-clause</dt><dd class="rpt-mono">LST-AVAIL-HIDDEN-SHOWN</dd></div><div Simulated corpus; company-agnostic prototype."/><script src="/_next/static/chunks/0cz1d0mv5g_q7.js" noModule=""></script></head><body><div hidden=""><!--$--><!--/$--></div><a href="#main-content" class="ds-skip">Skip to main content</a><nav aria-label="Primary" class="site-nav"><div class="site-nav-in"><a class="site-brand" href="/"><svg class="site-brand-mark" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M10 2.2l6.4 2.4v4.2c0 4.2-2.7 6.7-6.4 8.4-3.7-1.7-6.4-4.2-6.4-8.4V4.6z"></path><path d="M6.8 10.2l2.2 2.2 4.4-4.6"></path></svg>Curbside Commons</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/console">Console</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/report">Report</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/demo">Demo</a><a aria-current="page" class="site-navlink" href="/eval">Eval / Quality</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/metrics">Metrics</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/audit">Audit</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/cost">Cost</a><span class="site-status"><span class="site-status-pip" aria-hidden="true"></span>Prototype · REPLAY · $0.00</span></div></nav><div id="main-content" tabindex="-1"><main class="ds-data ds-wrap ds-view"><h1>Eval / Quality</h1><p class="ds-lead plain"><b>In plain terms:</b>every drafted message is scored before a human ever sees it — is it well-formed, do its declared claims all check out against this merchant&#x27;s data, and does it avoid forbidden promises?</p><p class="ds-lead tech"><b>Technically:</b>deterministic graders over the draft contract (structure · state-consistency · policy · no-leakage). They share the gate&#x27;s rule definitions; their teeth are proven by paired corrupted-record tests (a grader that can&#x27;t fail is theater) — including on the recorded real-Gemini drafts, where no-leakage catches a raw enum / risk-level leak the other dimensions missed.</p><div class="ds-note warn">These corpus scores grade the <b>deterministic stub</b> output. The same graders also scored a<!-- --> <b>recorded real Gemini run</b> — shown below (key-gated, $0.0042 spent) — so this stays honest about real output. The public <b>demo itself makes no live calls</b>.</div><section class="ds-stats c5"><div class="ds-stat accent"><div class="v">20<!-- -->/<!-- -->20</div><div class="l">drafts pass all dimensions</div></div><div class="ds-stat"><div class="v">20<!-- -->/<!-- -->20</div><div class="l">structure</div></div><div class="ds-stat"><div class="v">20<!-- -->/<!-- -->20</div><div class="l">state-consistency</div></div><div class="ds-stat"><div class="v">20<!-- -->/<!-- -->20</div><div class="l">policy</div></div><div class="ds-stat"><div class="v">20<!-- -->/<!-- -->/<!-- -->4</td></tr></tbody></table></div></section><section><h2 class="ds-h2-row">Recorded Gemini run — static fixture<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:400;color:var(--muted);font-family:var(--ff-sans);font-size:14px">(<!-- -->gemini-2.5-flash<!-- -->, <!-- -->2026-06-20<!-- -->)</span></h2><p class="ds-runline" style="max-width:78ch">A <b style="color:var(--ink-2)">frozen recording</b> of a local Gemini API run (one merchant per blocker). The public demo does <b style="color:var(--ink-2)">not</b> <!-- -->re-run or independently verify it (REPLAY-only, zero spend) — reproduce it yourself with your own key:<!-- --> <code class="ds-code">node --env-file=.env node_modules/.bin/vitest run evals/live-smoke.test.ts</code>. Total cost:<!-- --> <span class="ds-num">$<!-- -->0.0042</span> (cap $5). Modes: <!-- -->5 LIVE_AI · 1 FAILED_TO_FALLBACK<!-- -->. Gate: <!-- -->4 WARN · 2 PASS<!-- -->.</p><div class="ds-note" style="margin-top:14px"><div class="ds-tag role" style="background:none;border:none;padding:0;margin-bottom:8px">What the live run showed (honest)</div><ul style="margin:0;padding-left:18px;font-size:12.5px;line-height:1.7"><li>Claims-gatekeeper passed every DECLARED claim against merchant data on every produced draft (its core function held on live output).</li><li>AUTHENTIC failure handling on real output: schema-constrained generation occasionally returns unparseable output -&gt; FAILED_TO_FALLBACK to the deterministic stub, with billed cost still recorded so the cumulative cap stays honest. High-risk drafts are held for human approval (WARN).</li><li>Live runs surfaced a guardrail OVER-match: the pinned core flagged phrases like &quot;business verification&quot;, &quot;menu to be uploaded&quot;, &quot;photos to complete&quot; regardless of tense, false-blocking truthful drafts. FIXED in the agent tier (lib/agents/state-consistency.ts — a completion word must follow the step noun via a done-auxiliary), pinned core/differential UNTOUCHED. Confirmed here: 0 false blocks.</li><li>A no-leakage / register grader (added after this recording; lib/agents/state-consistency.ts, shared by the eval AND the gatekeeper) re-scores the recorded MERCHANT-FACING prose and CATCHES a real register leak in 3 of the 6 drafts: Mission Masa surfaced the raw &quot;bank_verification_needed&quot; enum + a &quot;Medium risk&quot; disclosure; Fog City Tacos and Bayview Bistro disclosed an internal risk level. Those now score 3/4, the clean drafts 4/4 — an authentic catch on real model output, reproducible from the frozen bodies (evals/live-samples.test.ts). The runtime gatekeeper now also treats a register leak as a BLOCKING failure (a leaky draft is auto-rejected before the human gate); the recorded gate column above predates that check, so a fresh run would BLOCK these 3. The live prompt was tightened to prevent such leaks going forward.</li></ul></div><div class="ds-tbl"><table><thead><tr><th scope="col">Blocker</th><th scope="col">Mode</th><th scope="col">Gate</th><th scope="col">Eval</th><th scope="col">Cost</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td class="ds-mono" style="font-size:12px">business_verification_needed</td><td class="ds-mono" style="font-size:12px;color:var(--muted)">LIVE_AI</td><td>WARN</td><td class="ds-mono">3/4</td><td class="ds-mono" style="color:var(--muted)">$<!-- -->0.000775</td></tr><tr><td class="ds-mono" style="font-size:12px">menu_upload_needed</td><td class="ds-mono" style="font-size:12px;color:var(--muted)">LIVE_AI</td><td>WARN</td><td class="ds-mono">3/4</td><td class="ds-mono" out/demo.html:1:<!DOCTYPE html><html lang="en" data-scroll-behavior="smooth" class="geist_f880305f-module__AJLRcW__variable geist_mono_42eec60-module__Gb1_vG__variable jetbrains_mono_6104e6b7-module__s6uLTa__variable"><head><meta charSet="utf-8"/><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1"/><link rel="preload" href="/_next/static/media/70bc3e132a0a741e-s.p.3t6q91iet4nsy.woff2" as="font" crossorigin="" type="font/woff2"/><link rel="preload" href="/_next/static/media/797e433ab948586e-s.p.0r6juujl39pe6.woff2" as="font" crossorigin="" type="font/woff2"/><link rel="preload" href="/_next/static/media/caa3a2e1cccd8315-s.p.0wgildi0cnwt9.woff2" as="font" crossorigin="" type="font/woff2"/><link rel="stylesheet" href="/_next/static/chunks/1tc2hl3z9iry_.css" data-precedence="next"/><link rel="preload" as="script" fetchPriority="low" href="/_next/static/chunks/10qk6v6416kh8.js"/><script src="/_next/static/chunks/27jktro2p5rq9.js" async=""></script><script src="/_next/static/chunks/2nykiepra7i1k.js" async=""></script><script src="/_next/static/chunks/273tk-n0d__2s.js" async=""></script><script src="/_next/static/chunks/turbopack-1d2m_hmbgvnw2.js" async=""></script><script src="/_next/static/chunks/0cgk26qnkn56x.js" async=""></script><script src="/_next/static/chunks/14mrh2-p_w84d.js" async=""></script><meta name="next-size-adjust" content=""/><title>Verifier demo — spec-faithful agent vs a false surface (simulated) · Curbside Commons</title><meta name="description" content="A scripted, deterministic walkthrough: a spec-faithful simulated agent follows a spec-valid but false serving copy; the verifier catches the surface/SOR mismatch. Simulated test data; zero AI calls; ends at item selection (no checkout)."/><script src="/_next/static/chunks/0cz1d0mv5g_q7.js" noModule=""></script></head><body><div hidden=""><!--$--><!--/$--></div><a href="#main-content" class="ds-skip">Skip to main content</a><nav aria-label="Primary" class="site-nav"><div class="site-nav-in"><a class="site-brand" href="/"><svg class="site-brand-mark" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M10 2.2l6.4 2.4v4.2c0 4.2-2.7 6.7-6.4 8.4-3.7-1.7-6.4-4.2-6.4-8.4V4.6z"></path><path d="M6.8 10.2l2.2 2.2 4.4-4.6"></path></svg>Curbside Commons</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/console">Console</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/report">Report</a><a aria-current="page" class="site-navlink" href="/demo">Demo</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/eval">Eval / Quality</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/metrics">Metrics</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/audit">Audit</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/cost">Cost</a><span class="site-status"><span class="site-status-pip" aria-hidden="true"></span>Prototype · REPLAY · $0.00</span></div></nav><div id="main-content" tabindex="-1"><main class="rpt-wrap dmo-wrap" id="demo"><div class="rpt-sim" role="note"><span class="rpt-sim-tag">SIMULATED</span><span class="rpt-sim-text">Synthetic test data — an invented restaurant, invented menu, invented prices. Not real DoorDash / Square / Uber Eats / Grubhub data, access, or business impact. The verification rules and the pinned data-format standard are real; the restaurant, its menu, and its records are invented.</span></div><header class="rpt-head"><p class="rpt-eyebrow">Verifier demo · listings truth check</p><h1 class="rpt-title">a spec-faithful simulated agent follows a spec-valid but false surface; the verifier catches the surface/SOR mismatch</h1><p class="rpt-intro">The comparison is exact, deterministic logic — no language model runs in this demo, and no live platform is touched.</p><dl class="rpt-meta dmo-meta"><div class="rpt-mrow"><dt>actor</dt><dd>spec-faithful demonstration actor — simulated</dd></div><div class="rpt-mrow"><dt>spec version</dt><dd class="rpt-mono">taxonomy-v1+acp-extract-2026-07-02+ucp-pin-2026-04-08</dd></div><div class="rpt-mrow"><dt>data</dt><dd class="rpt-mono">simulated: <!-- -->true</dd></div></dl></header><div class="dmo-beats"><section class="dmo-beat" aria-label="The agent reads the published serving copy — spec-faithful demonstration actor — simulated"><div class="dmo-beat-head"><span class="dmo-beat-no">Beat <!-- -->1</span><h2 class="dmo-beat-title">The agent reads the published serving copy — spec-faithful demonstration actor — simulated</h2></div><p class="dmo-beat-plain">A shopping agent reads the published menu feed — the same data any AI assistant would consume — and never sees the restaurant&#x27;s own records.</p><ul class="dmo-beat-lines"><li>intent: Order the &quot;Smoked Brisket Plate&quot; if the published feed lists it as available.</li><li>source read: the published ACP serving copy only (no system-of-record access)</li></ul></section><section class="dmo-beat" aria-label="The agent selects an item, trusting the surface"><div class="dmo-beat-head"><span class="dmo-beat-no">Beat <!-- -->2</span><h2 class="dmo-beat-title">The agent selects an item, trusting the surface</h2></div><p class="dmo-beat-plain">Trusting the feed at face value, the agent picks its target item and is ready to order it at the price the surface shows.</p><ul class="dmo-beat-lines"><li>selected item: &quot;Smoked Brisket Plate&quot; (row id item-006-v1)</li><li>read off the surface: price 12.00 USD · availability in_stock</li><li>the agent is now ready to order at that price — the demo stops here (no checkout).</li></ul><div class="dmo-verdicts"><span class="dmo-verdict ok">SELECTED (from the surface)</span></div></section><section class="dmo-beat" aria-label="The verifier checks that same copy against the records"><div class="dmo-beat-head"><span class="dmo-beat-no">Beat <!-- -->3</span><h2 class="dmo-beat-title">The verifier checks that same copy against the records</h2></div><p class="dmo-beat-plain">The verifier checks the exact same feed against the restaurant&#x27;s system-of-record and flags what the agent had no way to see.</p><ul class="dmo-beat-lines"><li>1 finding for the selected item (&quot;Smoked Brisket Plate&quot;); full report: 16 findings across the whole copy.</li></ul><div class="dmo-verdicts"><span class="dmo-verdict flag">DRIFT ON THE SELECTED ITEM — the agent could not have seen it</span></div><ol class="rpt-findings dmo-findings"><li class="rpt-finding"><div class="rpt-finding-lead"><span class="rpt-idx">01</span><p class="rpt-plain">The served price 12.00 does not match the catalog price 10.00.</p><span class="rpt-sev error">error</span></div><dl class="rpt-receipts" aria-label="evidence"><div class="rpt-rc"><dt>claim</dt><dd><span class="rpt-mono">item-006-v1#price.amount</span><span class="rpt-mono-sub">acp-feed<!-- --> · <!-- -->price.amount<!-- --> = <!-- -->12.00</span></dd></div><div class="rpt-rc"><dt>reference row</dt><dd class="rpt-mono">item-006-v1</dd></div><div class="rpt-rc"><dt>rule / spec-clause</dt><dd class="rpt-mono">LST-PRICE-VALUE</dd></div><div class="rpt-rc"><dt>class</dt><dd class="rpt-mono">price</dd></div></dl></li></ol></section><section class="dmo-beat" aria-label="Conformance-foil: spec-valid is not the same as true"><div class="dmo-beat-head"><span class="dmo-beat-no">Beat <!-- -->4</span><h2 class="dmo-beat-title">Conformance-foil: spec-valid is not the same as true</h2></div><p class="dmo-beat-plain">The same document passes the official schema check — it is correctly shaped — and still misstates the price versus the records.</p><ul class="dmo-beat-lines"><li>passes the official schema check; still lies — conformance: PASS (spec-shape); truth: 1 finding(s) vs the system-of-record.</li></ul><div class="dmo-verdicts"><span class="dmo-verdict ok">CONFORMANT (spec-valid)</span><span class="dmo-verdict flag">FALSE vs SOR (still lies)</span></div><ol class="rpt-findings dmo-findings"><li class="rpt-finding"><div class="rpt-finding-lead"><span class="rpt-idx">01</span><p class="rpt-plain">The served price 23.50 does not match the catalog price 21.50.</p><span class="rpt-sev error">error</span></div><dl class="rpt-receipts" aria-label="evidence"><div class="rpt-rc"><dt>claim</dt><dd><span class="rpt-mono">item-001-v1#price.amount</span><span class="rpt-mono-sub">ucp-catalog<!-- --> · <!-- -->price.amount<!-- --> = <!-- -->23.50</span></dd></div><div class="rpt-rc"><dt>reference row</dt><dd class="rpt-mono">item-001-v1</dd></div><div class="rpt-rc"><dt>rule / spec-clause</dt><dd class="rpt-mono">LST-PRICE-VALUE</dd></div><div class="rpt-rc"><dt>class</dt><dd class="rpt-mono">price</dd></div></dl></li></ol></section></div><footer class="rpt-foot"><p>The agent read only the published feed and never saw the restaurant’s records; the verifier checked that same feed against those records and cited every catch with its four receipts. No language model runs in this demo — the comparison is exact, deterministic logic. Simulated prototype, run on demand — not a live service, no real platform access. Human-led, AI-assisted, professionally reviewed.</p></footer></main><!--$--><!--/$--></div><footer class="site-footer"><div class="site-footer-in"><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Demo / portfolio prototype.</span> <!-- -->REPLAY over <span style="font-weight:600">fictional display names</span> +<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">synthetic activation state</span>(the adapter ingests real public DataSF records; the demo shows invented ones) — not production logs, real sends, real marketplace access, or real-impact data. The “real Gemini” output shown is a<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">recorded static fixture</span> (reproduce it locally with your own key). Human-led, AI-assisted, professionally reviewed.<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to</span> <!-- -->DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, DataSF, or any named business.</div></footer><script src="/_next/static/chunks/10qk6v6416kh8.js" id="_R_" diagnosis</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">How stuck this merchant is, and exactly what&#x27;s blocking them — by an auditable rule, not a model guess.</p><dl class="ds-fields"><div class="ds-field"><dt>Risk score</dt><dd>148 (High)</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Blocker</dt><dd>bank_verification_needed</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Next best action</dt><dd>verify_bank</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Days since signup</dt><dd>39</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Last login (days ago)</dt><dd>20</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Reason codes</dt><dd>inactivity, tenure_pressure</dd></div></dl><p class="ds-formula">risk = 2×<!-- -->39<!-- --> + 3×<!-- -->20<!-- --> + 10×(<!-- -->5<!-- -->−<!-- -->4<!-- -->) = <!-- -->148<span class="dim">  <!-- --># <!-- -->1<!-- --> step<!-- --> remaining</span></p><div class="ds-sub"><div class="ds-tags"><span class="ds-tag role">Why they&#x27;re stuck</span><span class="ds-tag">dormant</span><span class="ds-tag">merchant side</span></div><p class="ds-card-p" style="margin-top:10px">Made progress then went inactive — likely lost momentum/motivation; re-engage before the banking / payout nudge.</p><div style="margin-top:10px;border:1px solid var(--rule);background:var(--bg);border-radius:8px;padding:10px 12px"><span class="ds-tag role" style="background:none;border:none;padding:0">Recommended play ·<!-- --> </span><span style="font-size:12px;font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">re engagement</span><p class="ds-card-p" style="margin-top:6px">Re-engage acknowledging their earlier progress, THEN: Send &#x27;add or verify your bank to get paid&#x27;; escalate to ops if it&#x27;s a Stripe fraud hold.</p><p class="ds-card-tech" style="font-family:var(--ff-sans)">Advanced through onboarding, then went inactive — momentum lost; re-engage before the step nudge (a step-only nudge to a quiet merchant under-performs).</p></div><p class="ds-gate-note" style="margin-top:8px">Engagement state is a snapshot inference (last_login × steps × tenure), not a measured event. Pinning the exact root cause needs instrumentation: bank_verification_status / fraud_hold_flag.</p></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>2 · Drafted outreach</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A bounded, schema-constrained draft. Here it&#x27;s the deterministic stub (REPLAY); a recorded real-Gemini run is on the Eval page — the safety machinery around it is identical either way.</p><div class="ds-draft"><div class="ds-draft-sub">Your next onboarding step with Curbside Commons</div><div class="ds-draft-body">Hi Cable Car Goods, thanks for getting started on your Curbside Commons onboarding.
