stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span>Hi Cable Car Goods, thanks for getting started on your Curbside Commons onboarding.<span class="ev"> <!-- -->→ <code class="ds-code">merchant_name</code></span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span>Your next step is to verify your bank information.<span class="ev"> <!-- -->→ <code class="ds-code">steps_completed</code></span></span></li></ul><p class="ds-meta-line">mode: <!-- -->DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE<!-- --> · model: <!-- -->deterministic-judge<!-- --> · cost: $<!-- -->0.00</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>5 · Domain quality check (domain judge)</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A third, independent check — on a different question than faithfulness. Not &#x27;is every fact true?&#x27; but &#x27;is this a GOOD activation message?&#x27; — scored against a cited rubric: matched to the merchant&#x27;s real blocker · the right play for their engagement state · no over-promising. It&#x27;s advisory and recall-favoring: the verdict is surfaced for the reviewer and recorded in the audit trail, but it never changes the send — eligibility and the human approval gate stay deterministic (a low-risk draft can still be simulated-sent even when flagged). Here BOTH the draft and this verdict are deterministic $0 stubs (REPLAY) — a minimal stub nudge often trips the engagement-fit check, which is the tertiary control doing its job, not the product grading its real output down; the live cross-family judge (Groq gpt-oss-120b) and the real drafter are separate and key-gated.</p><div class="ds-judge-h"><span class="ds-chip warn"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg>FLAGGED FOR REVIEW</span><span class="ds-judge-count">2<!-- -->/<!-- -->3<!-- --> domain-quality dimensions passed</span></div><ul class="ds-judge-list"><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Matched to the merchant&#x27;s actual blocker</span><span class="ev"> — <!-- -->Addresses the &#x27;Banking / payout&#x27; blocker the merchant is stuck on.</span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--warn)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg><span><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Right play for the engagement state</span><span class="ev"> — <!-- -->Merchant is &#x27;dormant&#x27; but the draft is a bare step nudge with no re-engagement/value framing.</span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">No over-promising (incl. implied / typicality claims)</span><span class="ev"> — <!-- -->No unsubstantiated outcome / implied-typicality language detected.</span></span></li></ul><p class="ds-meta-line">mode: <!-- -->DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE<!-- --> · model: <!-- -->deterministic-domain-judge<!-- --> · cost: $<!-- -->0.00<!-- --> · advisory — does not change the send decision</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>6 · Eval / quality</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">An independent measurement of draft quality across four dimensions — the deep-AI showcase, in human terms.</p><div class="ds-grid g4" style="margin-top:0"><div style="border:1px solid var(--rule);border-radius:10px;padding:12px 14px"><div style="display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;gap:8px"><span style="font-size:13px;font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">structure</span><span class="ds-verdict ok"><svg 5.5"></path></svg>PASS</span></div></div><div style="border:1px solid var(--rule);border-radius:10px;padding:12px 14px"><div style="display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;gap:8px"><span style="font-size:13px;font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">policy</span><span class="ds-verdict ok"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg>PASS</span></div></div><div style="font-family:var(--ff-sans);font-size:12px">4<!-- -->/<!-- -->4<!-- --> dimensions passing.</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>7 · Human-in-the-loop gate</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A person decides — hold, reject, or send. Low-risk, clean drafts are eligible to send (simulated); high-risk ones are held for approval.</p><div><div class="ds-held"><div class="h"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg>Held for human approval (<!-- -->high_risk<!-- -->).</div><div class="p">No message is sent until a human approves.</div></div><div class="ds-gate-btns"><button type="button" disabled="" class="ds-btn primary">Approve &amp; send</button><button type="button" disabled="" class="ds-btn" style="color:var(--error);border-color:rgba(217, 45, 32, 0.4)">Reject</button><button type="button" disabled="" class="ds-btn">Hold</button></div><p class="ds-gate-note">Display of the gate state. Interactive approval (writing the decision back) lands in Phase C.</p></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>8 · Audit trail</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">Every step of the decision, recorded.</p><ol class="ds-audit"><li><span class="a-actor">system</span><span class="a-action">TRIAGE</span><span class="a-detail">risk=148 (High); blocker=bank_verification_needed; engagement=dormant; play=re_engagement</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">draft</span><span class="a-action">DETERMINISTIC_RULES</span><span class="a-detail">Outreach drafted by the deterministic stub (REPLAY; a recorded real-Gemini run is on the Eval page).</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">gatekeeper</span><span class="a-action">WARN</span><span class="a-detail">0 failure(s), 1 warning(s).</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">judge</span><span class="a-action">DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE</span><span class="a-detail">3/3 prose assertions supported by merchant data; all supported.</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">domain</span><span class="a-action">DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE</span><span class="a-detail">2/3 domain-quality dimensions passed → domain quality flagged (advisory — surfaced for review; does not change the send or eligibility).</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">eval</span><span class="a-action">PASS</span><span class="a-detail">4/4 quality dimensions passed.</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">system</span><span class="a-action">DRAFTED</span><span class="a-detail">Held for human approval before any send.</span></li></ol></section></div><p class="ds-note warn" style="margin-top:24px">The merchant names shown are FICTIONAL (no real businesses — so synthetic risk states are never attached to real people). The product&#x27;s adapter ingests real DataSF public-record names (PDDL 1.0, public domain — name + category only, PII-scrubbed; see lib/ingest/sf-adapter.ts). Activation state is synthetic and illustrative. No real merchant relationship or account.</p></main><!--$--><!--/$--></div><footer class="site-footer"><div class="site-footer-in"><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Demo / portfolio prototype.</span> <!-- -->REPLAY over <span style="font-weight:600">fictional display names</span> +<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">synthetic activation state</span>(the adapter ingests real public DataSF records; the demo shows invented ones) — not production logs, real sends, real marketplace access, or real-impact data. diagnosis</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">How stuck this merchant is, and exactly what&#x27;s blocking them — by an auditable rule, not a model guess.</p><dl class="ds-fields"><div class="ds-field"><dt>Risk score</dt><dd>146 (High)</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Blocker</dt><dd>business_verification_needed</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Next best action</dt><dd>verify_business</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Days since signup</dt><dd>39</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Last login (days ago)</dt><dd>6</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Reason codes</dt><dd>low_completion, tenure_pressure</dd></div></dl><p class="ds-formula">risk = 2×<!-- -->39<!-- --> + 3×<!-- -->6<!-- --> + 10×(<!-- -->5<!-- -->−<!-- -->0<!-- -->) = <!-- -->146<span class="dim">  <!-- --># <!-- -->5<!-- --> step<!-- -->s<!-- --> remaining</span></p><div class="ds-sub"><div class="ds-tags"><span class="ds-tag role">Why they&#x27;re stuck</span><span class="ds-tag">progressing</span><span class="ds-tag">merchant side</span></div><p class="ds-card-p" style="margin-top:10px">Engaged but blocked at business / identity verification; the precise cause needs the verification_status / business_info_match_flag (IRS/Stripe mismatch vs not-yet-submitted) signal.</p><div style="margin-top:10px;border:1px solid var(--rule);background:var(--bg);border-radius:8px;padding:10px 12px"><span class="ds-tag role" style="background:none;border:none;padding:0">Recommended play ·<!-- --> </span><span style="font-size:12px;font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">self serve nudge</span><p class="ds-card-p" style="margin-top:6px">Send the &#x27;match your business name + tax ID to your IRS records&#x27; fix with the exact field to correct.</p><p class="ds-card-tech" style="font-family:var(--ff-sans)">Engaged and progressing — a step-matched self-serve nudge keeps momentum.</p></div><p class="ds-gate-note" style="margin-top:8px">Engagement state is a snapshot inference (last_login × steps × tenure), not a measured event. Pinning the exact root cause needs instrumentation: verification_status / business_info_match_flag (IRS/Stripe mismatch vs not-yet-submitted).</p></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>2 · Drafted outreach</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A bounded, schema-constrained draft. style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A third, independent check — on a different question than faithfulness. Not &#x27;is every fact true?&#x27; but &#x27;is this a GOOD activation message?&#x27; — scored against a cited rubric: matched to the merchant&#x27;s real blocker · the right play for their engagement state · no over-promising. It&#x27;s advisory and recall-favoring: the verdict is surfaced for the reviewer and recorded in the audit trail, but it never changes the send — eligibility and the human approval gate stay deterministic (a low-risk draft can still be simulated-sent even when flagged). Here BOTH the draft and this verdict are deterministic $0 stubs (REPLAY) — a minimal stub nudge often trips the engagement-fit check, which is the tertiary control doing its job, not the product grading its real output down; the live cross-family judge (Groq gpt-oss-120b) and the real drafter are separate and key-gated.</p><div class="ds-judge-h"><span class="ds-chip pass"><svg viewBox="0 0 style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A person decides — hold, reject, or send. Low-risk, clean drafts are eligible to send (simulated); high-risk ones are held for approval.</p><div><div class="ds-held"><div class="h"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg>Held for human approval (<!-- -->high_risk<!-- -->).</div><div class="p">No message is sent until a human approves.</div></div><div class="ds-gate-btns"><button type="button" disabled="" class="ds-btn primary">Approve &amp; send</button><button type="button" disabled="" class="ds-btn" style="color:var(--error);border-color:rgba(217, 45, 32, 0.4)">Reject</button><button type="button" disabled="" class="ds-btn">Hold</button></div><p class="ds-gate-note">Display of the gate state. Interactive approval (writing the decision back) lands in Phase C.</p></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>8 · Audit trail</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">Every step of the decision, recorded.</p><ol class="ds-audit"><li><span class="a-actor">system</span><span class="a-action">TRIAGE</span><span class="a-detail">risk=146 (High); blocker=business_verification_needed; engagement=progressing; play=self_serve_nudge</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">draft</span><span class="a-action">DETERMINISTIC_RULES</span><span class="a-detail">Outreach drafted by the deterministic stub (REPLAY; a recorded real-Gemini run is on the Eval page).</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">gatekeeper</span><span class="a-action">WARN</span><span class="a-detail">0 failure(s), 1 warning(s).</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">judge</span><span class="a-action">DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE</span><span class="a-detail">3/3 prose assertions supported by merchant data; all supported.</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">domain</span><span class="a-action">DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE</span><span class="a-detail">3/3 domain-quality dimensions passed → good activation practice.</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">eval</span><span class="a-action">PASS</span><span class="a-detail">4/4 quality dimensions passed.</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">system</span><span class="a-action">DRAFTED</span><span class="a-detail">Held for human approval before any send.</span></li></ol></section></div><p class="ds-note warn" style="margin-top:24px">The merchant names shown are FICTIONAL (no real businesses — so synthetic risk states are never attached to real people). The product&#x27;s adapter ingests real DataSF public-record names (PDDL 1.0, public domain — name + category only, PII-scrubbed; see lib/ingest/sf-adapter.ts). Activation state is synthetic and illustrative. No real merchant relationship or account.</p></main><!--$--><!--/$--></div><footer class="site-footer"><div class="site-footer-in"><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Demo / portfolio prototype.</span> <!-- -->REPLAY over <span style="font-weight:600">fictional display names</span> +<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">synthetic activation state</span>(the adapter ingests real public DataSF records; the demo shows invented ones) — not production logs, real sends, real marketplace access, or real-impact data. The “real Gemini” output shown is a<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">recorded static fixture</span> (reproduce it locally with your own key). Simulated corpus; company-agnostic prototype."/><script src="/_next/static/chunks/0cz1d0mv5g_q7.js" noModule=""></script></head><body><div hidden=""><!--$--><!--/$--></div><a href="#main-content" class="ds-skip">Skip to main content</a><nav aria-label="Primary" class="site-nav"><div class="site-nav-in"><a class="site-brand" href="/"><svg class="site-brand-mark" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M10 2.2l6.4 2.4v4.2c0 4.2-2.7 6.7-6.4 8.4-3.7-1.7-6.4-4.2-6.4-8.4V4.6z"></path><path d="M6.8 10.2l2.2 2.2 4.4-4.6"></path></svg>Curbside Commons</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/console">Console</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/report">Report</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/demo">Demo</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/eval">Eval / Quality</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/metrics">Metrics</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/audit">Audit</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/cost">Cost</a><span class="site-status"><span class="site-status-pip" aria-hidden="true"></span>Prototype · REPLAY · $0.00</span></div></nav><div id="main-content" tabindex="-1"><main class="ds-data ds-wrap ds-narrow ds-view"><a class="ds-back" href="/"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M14 10H4 M8.4 5.8L4 10l4.4 4.2"></path></svg><span>Activation queue</span></a><div class="ds-mhead"><div><h1>Embarcadero Eats</h1><p class="ds-msub">Restaurant<!-- --> · <!-- -->M006<!-- --> · onboarding <!-- -->5<!-- -->/<!-- -->5</p></div><span class="ds-chip high" style="font-size:11px">High<!-- --> risk · <!-- -->85</span></div><div class="ds-stack" style="margin-top:18px"><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>1 · Triage &amp; diagnosis</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">How stuck this merchant is, and exactly what&#x27;s blocking them — by an auditable rule, not a model guess.</p><dl class="ds-fields"><div class="ds-field"><dt>Risk score</dt><dd>85 (High)</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Blocker</dt><dd>final_verification_needed</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Next best action</dt><dd>complete_final_verification</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Days since signup</dt><dd>17</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Last login (days ago)</dt><dd>17</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Reason codes</dt><dd>inactivity, tenure_pressure</dd></div></dl><p class="ds-formula">risk = 2×<!-- -->17<!-- --> + 3×<!-- -->17<!-- --> + 10×(<!-- -->5<!-- -->−<!-- -->5<!-- -->) = <!-- -->85<span class="dim">  <!-- --># <!-- -->0<!-- --> step<!-- -->s<!-- --> remaining</span></p><div class="ds-sub"><div class="ds-tags"><span class="ds-tag role">Why they&#x27;re stuck</span><span class="ds-tag">dormant</span><span class="ds-tag">merchant side</span></div><p class="ds-card-p" style="margin-top:10px">Made progress then went inactive — likely lost momentum/motivation; re-engage before the final verification nudge.</p><div style="margin-top:10px;border:1px solid var(--rule);background:var(--bg);border-radius:8px;padding:10px 12px"><span class="ds-tag role" style="background:none;border:none;padding:0">Recommended play ·<!-- --> </span><span style="font-size:12px;font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">re engagement</span><p class="ds-card-p" style="margin-top:6px">Re-engage acknowledging their earlier progress, THEN: Nudge the final review step that flips the store live.</p><p class="ds-card-tech" style="font-family:var(--ff-sans)">Advanced through onboarding, then went inactive — momentum lost; re-engage before the step nudge (a step-only nudge to a quiet merchant under-performs).</p></div><p class="ds-gate-note" style="margin-top:8px">Engagement state is a snapshot inference (last_login × steps × tenure), not a measured event. Pinning the exact root cause needs instrumentation: final_review_status.</p></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>2 · Drafted outreach</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A bounded, schema-constrained draft. Here it&#x27;s the deterministic stub (REPLAY); a recorded real-Gemini run is on the Eval page — the safety machinery around it is identical either way.</p><div class="ds-draft"><div class="ds-draft-sub">Your next onboarding step with Curbside Commons</div><div class="ds-draft-body">Hi Embarcadero Eats, thanks for getting started on your Curbside Commons onboarding. Your next step is to complete your final verification. Reply to this message if you would like a hand.</div></div><p class="ds-card-tech" style="font-family:var(--ff-sans);color:var(--graphite)">Claims (each declared claim, verified against the merchant&#x27;s data):</p><ul class="ds-claims"><li><svg style="width:13px;height:13px;color:var(--ok);flex:none" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><code class="ds-code">steps_completed<!-- --> = <!-- -->5</code><span style="color:var(--muted)">→ merchant.<!-- -->steps_completed<!-- --> = <!-- -->5</span></li><li><svg style="width:13px;height:13px;color:var(--ok);flex:none" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><code class="ds-code">total_steps<!-- --> = <!-- -->5</code><span style="color:var(--muted)">→ merchant.<!-- -->total_steps<!-- --> = <!-- -->5</span></li><li><svg style="width:13px;height:13px;color:var(--ok);flex:none" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><code class="ds-code">current_blocker_code<!-- --> = <!-- -->final_verification_needed</code><span style="color:var(--muted)">→ merchant.<!-- -->current_blocker_code<!-- --> = <!-- -->final_verification_needed</span></li><li><svg style="width:13px;height:13px;color:var(--ok);flex:none" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><code class="ds-code">next_best_action<!-- --> = <!-- -->complete_final_verification</code><span style="color:var(--muted)">→ merchant.<!-- -->next_best_action<!-- --> = <!-- -->complete_final_verification</span></li></ul><p class="ds-meta-line">mode: <!-- -->DETERMINISTIC_RULES<!-- --> · cost: $<!-- -->0.00<!-- --> · model: <!-- -->stub-deterministic.v1</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>3 · Claims-gatekeeper</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A deterministic firewall: the draft cannot reach a human unless every declared claim checks out against the merchant&#x27;s data and no forbidden-claim pattern is present.</p><div class="ds-judge-h"><span class="ds-chip warn"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg>WARN</span><span class="ds-judge-count">Approved for the human gate</span></div><div class="ds-flaglist" style="color:var(--ink-2)"><span class="fl-l">Guardrail flags<!-- -->: </span><span style="color:var(--muted)">none — clean</span></div><div class="ds-flaglist" style="color:var(--ink-2)"><span class="fl-l">Failures<!-- -->: </span><span style="color:var(--muted)">none</span></div><div class="ds-flaglist" style="color:var(--ink-2)"><span class="fl-l">Warnings<!-- -->: </span><ul class="ds-flaglist warn"><li>held for human review (high_risk)</li></ul></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>4 · Faithfulness check (semantic judge)</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A second, independent check: an LLM from a DIFFERENT model family reads the finished message and verifies each factual sentence against the merchant&#x27;s data row — catching an invented number, capability, or timeline the deterministic gatekeeper structurally can&#x27;t see. Here it&#x27;s the deterministic stub verdict (REPLAY, $0); the live cross-family judge (Groq gpt-oss-120b) is key-gated.</p><div class="ds-judge-h"><span class="ds-chip pass"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg>ALL SUPPORTED</span><span class="ds-judge-count">3<!-- -->/<!-- -->3<!-- --> <!-- -->prose assertions backed by the data row</span></div><ul class="ds-judge-list"><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span>Your next onboarding step with Curbside Commons.<span class="ev"> <!-- -->→ <code class="ds-code">steps_completed</code></span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span>Hi Embarcadero Eats, thanks for getting started on your Curbside Commons onboarding.<span class="ev"> <!-- -->→ <code class="ds-code">merchant_name</code></span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span>Your next step is to complete your final verification.<span class="ev"> <!-- -->→ <code class="ds-code">steps_completed</code></span></span></li></ul><p class="ds-meta-line">mode: <!-- -->DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE<!-- --> · model: <!-- -->deterministic-judge<!-- --> · cost: $<!-- -->0.00</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>5 · Domain quality check (domain judge)</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A third, independent check — on a different question than faithfulness. Not &#x27;is every fact true?&#x27; but &#x27;is this a GOOD activation message?&#x27; — scored against a cited rubric: matched to the merchant&#x27;s real blocker · the right play for their engagement state · no over-promising. It&#x27;s advisory and recall-favoring: the verdict is surfaced for the reviewer and recorded in the audit trail, but it never changes the send — eligibility and the human approval gate stay deterministic (a low-risk draft can still be simulated-sent even when flagged). Here BOTH the draft and this verdict are deterministic $0 stubs (REPLAY) — a minimal stub nudge often trips the engagement-fit check, which is the tertiary control doing its job, not the product grading its real output down; the live cross-family judge (Groq gpt-oss-120b) and the real drafter are separate and key-gated.</p><div class="ds-judge-h"><span class="ds-chip warn"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg>FLAGGED FOR REVIEW</span><span class="ds-judge-count">2<!-- -->/<!-- -->3<!-- --> domain-quality dimensions passed</span></div><ul class="ds-judge-list"><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Matched to the merchant&#x27;s actual blocker</span><span class="ev"> — <!-- -->Addresses the &#x27;Final verification&#x27; blocker the merchant is stuck on.</span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--warn)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg><span><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Right play for the engagement state</span><span class="ev"> — <!-- -->Merchant is &#x27;dormant&#x27; but the draft is a bare step nudge with no re-engagement/value framing.</span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">No over-promising (incl. implied / typicality claims)</span><span class="ev"> — <!-- -->No unsubstantiated outcome / implied-typicality language detected.</span></span></li></ul><p class="ds-meta-line">mode: <!-- -->DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE<!-- --> · model: <!-- -->deterministic-domain-judge<!-- --> · cost: $<!-- -->0.00<!-- --> · advisory — does not change the send decision</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>6 · Eval / quality</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">An independent measurement of draft quality across four dimensions — the deep-AI showcase, in human terms.</p><div class="ds-grid g4" style="margin-top:0"><div style="border:1px solid var(--rule);border-radius:10px;padding:12px 14px"><div style="display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;gap:8px"><span style="font-size:13px;font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">structure</span><span class="ds-verdict ok"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 style="display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;gap:8px"><span style="font-size:13px;font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">state-consistency</span><span class="ds-verdict ok"><svg 5.5"></path></svg>PASS</span></div></div><div style="border:1px solid var(--rule);border-radius:10px;padding:12px 14px"><div style="display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;gap:8px"><span style="font-size:13px;font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">policy</span><span class="ds-verdict ok"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg>PASS</span></div></div><div style="font-family:var(--ff-sans);font-size:12px">4<!-- -->/<!-- -->4<!-- --> dimensions passing.</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>7 · Human-in-the-loop gate</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A person decides — hold, reject, or send. Low-risk, clean drafts are eligible to send (simulated); high-risk ones are held for approval.</p><div><div class="ds-held"><div class="h"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg>Held for human approval (<!-- -->high_risk<!-- -->).</div><div class="p">No message is sent until a human approves.</div></div><div class="ds-gate-btns"><button type="button" disabled="" class="ds-btn primary">Approve &amp; send</button><button type="button" disabled="" class="ds-btn" style="color:var(--error);border-color:rgba(217, 45, 32, 0.4)">Reject</button><button type="button" disabled="" class="ds-btn">Hold</button></div><p class="ds-gate-note">Display of the gate state. Interactive approval (writing the decision back) lands in Phase C.</p></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>8 · Audit trail</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">Every step of the decision, recorded.</p><ol class="ds-audit"><li><span class="a-actor">system</span><span class="a-action">TRIAGE</span><span class="a-detail">risk=85 (High); blocker=final_verification_needed; engagement=dormant; play=re_engagement</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">draft</span><span class="a-action">DETERMINISTIC_RULES</span><span class="a-detail">Outreach drafted by the deterministic stub (REPLAY; a recorded real-Gemini run is on the Eval page).</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">gatekeeper</span><span class="a-action">WARN</span><span class="a-detail">0 failure(s), 1 warning(s).</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">judge</span><span class="a-action">DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE</span><span class="a-detail">3/3 prose assertions supported by merchant data; all supported.</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">domain</span><span class="a-action">DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE</span><span class="a-detail">2/3 domain-quality dimensions passed → domain quality flagged (advisory — surfaced for review; does not change the send or eligibility).</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">eval</span><span class="a-action">PASS</span><span class="a-detail">4/4 quality dimensions passed.</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">system</span><span class="a-action">DRAFTED</span><span class="a-detail">Held for human approval before any send.</span></li></ol></section></div><p class="ds-note warn" style="margin-top:24px">The merchant names shown are FICTIONAL (no real businesses — so synthetic risk states are never attached to real people). The product&#x27;s adapter ingests real DataSF public-record names (PDDL 1.0, public domain — name + category only, PII-scrubbed; see lib/ingest/sf-adapter.ts). Activation state is synthetic and illustrative. No real merchant relationship or account.</p></main><!--$--><!--/$--></div><footer class="site-footer"><div class="site-footer-in"><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Demo / portfolio prototype.</span> <!-- -->REPLAY over <span style="font-weight:600">fictional display names</span> +<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">synthetic activation state</span>(the adapter ingests real public DataSF records; the demo shows invented ones) — not production logs, real sends, real marketplace access, or real-impact data. No real merchant relationship or account.\"}]\n"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"b:[[\"$\",\"meta\",\"0\",{\"charSet\":\"utf-8\"}],[\"$\",\"meta\",\"1\",{\"name\":\"viewport\",\"content\":\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1\"}]]\n"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"8:null\nd:[[\"$\",\"title\",\"0\",{\"children\":\"Bernal Books · Curbside Commons\"}],[\"$\",\"meta\",\"1\",{\"name\":\"description\",\"content\":\"The truth layer for agentic commerce — a deterministic verifier of platform/AI-agent serving copies vs the merchant system-of-record, UCP conformance, and NYC fee-cap audit. Simulated corpus; company-agnostic prototype."/><script src="/_next/static/chunks/0cz1d0mv5g_q7.js" noModule=""></script></head><body><div hidden=""><!--$--><!--/$--></div><a href="#main-content" class="ds-skip">Skip to main content</a><nav aria-label="Primary" class="site-nav"><div class="site-nav-in"><a class="site-brand" href="/"><svg class="site-brand-mark" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M10 2.2l6.4 2.4v4.2c0 4.2-2.7 6.7-6.4 8.4-3.7-1.7-6.4-4.2-6.4-8.4V4.6z"></path><path d="M6.8 10.2l2.2 2.2 4.4-4.6"></path></svg>Curbside Commons</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/console">Console</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/report">Report</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/demo">Demo</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/eval">Eval / Quality</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/metrics">Metrics</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/audit">Audit</a><a class="site-navlink" href="/cost">Cost</a><span class="site-status"><span class="site-status-pip" aria-hidden="true"></span>Prototype · REPLAY · $0.00</span></div></nav><div id="main-content" tabindex="-1"><main class="ds-data ds-wrap ds-narrow ds-view"><a class="ds-back" href="/"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M14 10H4 M8.4 5.8L4 10l4.4 4.2"></path></svg><span>Activation queue</span></a><div class="ds-mhead"><div><h1>Haight Street Hardware</h1><p class="ds-msub">Retail<!-- --> · <!-- -->M013<!-- --> · onboarding <!-- -->0<!-- -->/<!-- -->5</p></div><span class="ds-chip high" style="font-size:11px">High<!-- --> risk · <!-- -->218</span></div><div class="ds-stack" style="margin-top:18px"><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>1 · Triage &amp; diagnosis</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">How stuck this merchant is, and exactly what&#x27;s blocking them — by an auditable rule, not a model guess.</p><dl class="ds-fields"><div class="ds-field"><dt>Risk score</dt><dd>218 (High)</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Blocker</dt><dd>business_verification_needed</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Next best action</dt><dd>verify_business</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Days since signup</dt><dd>39</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Last login (days ago)</dt><dd>30</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Reason codes</dt><dd>low_completion, inactivity, tenure_pressure</dd></div></dl><p class="ds-formula">risk = 2×<!-- -->39<!-- --> + 3×<!-- -->30<!-- --> + 10×(<!-- -->5<!-- -->−<!-- -->0<!-- -->) = <!-- -->218<span class="dim">  <!-- --># <!-- -->5<!-- --> step<!-- -->s<!-- --> remaining</span></p><div class="ds-sub"><div class="ds-tags"><span class="ds-tag role">Why they&#x27;re stuck</span><span class="ds-tag">ghosted</span><span class="ds-tag">merchant side</span></div><p class="ds-card-p" style="margin-top:10px">Stalled early and disengaged — likely a motivation/value gap, not a business / identity verification problem per se.</p><div style="margin-top:10px;border:1px solid var(--rule);background:var(--bg);border-radius:8px;padding:10px 12px"><span class="ds-tag role" style="background:none;border:none;padding:0">Recommended play ·<!-- --> </span><span style="font-size:12px;font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">re engagement</span><p class="ds-card-p" style="margin-top:6px">Re-engage first (value-prove + a time-limited reason to return), THEN: Send the &#x27;match your business name + tax ID to your IRS records&#x27; fix with the exact field to correct.</p><p class="ds-card-tech" style="font-family:var(--ff-sans)">Inactive + barely started = a motivation/value gap; a step-only nudge to a disengaged merchant fails (research: generic-vs-matched outreach).</p></div><p class="ds-gate-note" style="margin-top:8px">Engagement state is a snapshot inference (last_login × steps × tenure), not a measured event. Pinning the exact root cause needs instrumentation: verification_status / business_info_match_flag (IRS/Stripe mismatch vs not-yet-submitted).</p></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>2 · Drafted outreach</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A bounded, schema-constrained draft. Here it&#x27;s the deterministic stub (REPLAY); a recorded real-Gemini run is on the Eval page — the safety machinery around it is identical either way.</p><div class="ds-draft"><div class="ds-draft-sub">Your next onboarding step with Curbside Commons</div><div class="ds-draft-body">Hi Haight Street Hardware, thanks for getting started on your Curbside Commons onboarding. Your next step is to verify your business information. Reply to this message if you would like a hand.</div></div><p class="ds-card-tech" style="font-family:var(--ff-sans);color:var(--graphite)">Claims (each declared claim, verified against the merchant&#x27;s data):</p><ul class="ds-claims"><li><svg style="width:13px;height:13px;color:var(--ok);flex:none" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><code class="ds-code">steps_completed<!-- --> = <!-- -->0</code><span style="color:var(--muted)">→ merchant.<!-- -->steps_completed<!-- --> = <!-- -->0</span></li><li><svg style="width:13px;height:13px;color:var(--ok);flex:none" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><code class="ds-code">total_steps<!-- --> = <!-- -->5</code><span style="color:var(--muted)">→ merchant.<!-- -->total_steps<!-- --> = <!-- -->5</span></li><li><svg style="width:13px;height:13px;color:var(--ok);flex:none" viewBox="0 0 20 20" class="ds-code">current_blocker_code<!-- --> = <!-- -->business_verification_needed</code><span style="color:var(--muted)">→ merchant.<!-- -->current_blocker_code<!-- --> = <!-- -->business_verification_needed</span></li><li><svg style="width:13px;height:13px;color:var(--ok);flex:none" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><code class="ds-code">next_best_action<!-- --> = <!-- -->verify_business</code><span style="color:var(--muted)">→ merchant.<!-- -->next_best_action<!-- --> = <!-- -->verify_business</span></li></ul><p class="ds-meta-line">mode: <!-- -->DETERMINISTIC_RULES<!-- --> · cost: $<!-- -->0.00<!-- --> · model: <!-- -->stub-deterministic.v1</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>3 · Claims-gatekeeper</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A deterministic firewall: the draft cannot reach a human unless every declared claim checks out against the merchant&#x27;s data and no forbidden-claim pattern is present.</p><div class="ds-judge-h"><span class="ds-chip warn"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg>WARN</span><span class="ds-judge-count">Approved for the human gate</span></div><div class="ds-flaglist" style="color:var(--ink-2)"><span class="fl-l">Guardrail flags<!-- -->: </span><span style="color:var(--muted)">none — clean</span></div><div class="ds-flaglist" style="color:var(--ink-2)"><span class="fl-l">Failures<!-- -->: </span><span style="color:var(--muted)">none</span></div><div class="ds-flaglist" style="color:var(--ink-2)"><span class="fl-l">Warnings<!-- -->: </span><ul class="ds-flaglist warn"><li>held for human review (high_risk)</li></ul></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>4 · Faithfulness check (semantic judge)</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A second, independent check: an LLM from a DIFFERENT model family reads the finished message and verifies each factual sentence against the merchant&#x27;s data row — catching an invented number, capability, or timeline the deterministic gatekeeper structurally can&#x27;t see. Here it&#x27;s the deterministic stub verdict (REPLAY, $0); the live cross-family judge (Groq gpt-oss-120b) is key-gated.</p><div class="ds-judge-h"><span class="ds-chip pass"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg>ALL SUPPORTED</span><span class="ds-judge-count">3<!-- -->/<!-- -->3<!-- --> <!-- -->prose assertions backed by the data row</span></div><ul class="ds-judge-list"><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span>Your next onboarding step with Curbside Commons.<span class="ev"> <!-- -->→ <code class="ds-code">steps_completed</code></span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span>Hi Haight Street Hardware, thanks for getting started on your Curbside Commons onboarding.<span class="ev"> <!-- -->→ <code class="ds-code">merchant_name</code></span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span>Your next step is to verify your business information.<span class="ev"> <!-- -->→ <code class="ds-code">steps_completed</code></span></span></li></ul><p class="ds-meta-line">mode: <!-- -->DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE<!-- --> · model: <!-- -->deterministic-judge<!-- --> · cost: $<!-- -->0.00</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>5 · Domain quality check (domain judge)</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A third, independent check — on a different question than faithfulness. Not &#x27;is every fact true?&#x27; but &#x27;is this a GOOD activation message?&#x27; — scored against a cited rubric: matched to the merchant&#x27;s real blocker · the right play for their engagement state · no over-promising. It&#x27;s advisory and recall-favoring: the verdict is surfaced for the reviewer and recorded in the audit trail, but it never changes the send — eligibility and the human approval gate stay deterministic (a low-risk draft can still be simulated-sent even when flagged). Here BOTH the draft and this verdict are deterministic $0 stubs (REPLAY) — a minimal stub nudge often trips the engagement-fit check, which is the tertiary control doing its job, not the product grading its real output down; the live cross-family judge (Groq gpt-oss-120b) and the real drafter are separate and key-gated.</p><div class="ds-judge-h"><span class="ds-chip warn"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg>FLAGGED FOR REVIEW</span><span class="ds-judge-count">2<!-- -->/<!-- -->3<!-- --> domain-quality dimensions passed</span></div><ul class="ds-judge-list"><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Matched to the merchant&#x27;s actual blocker</span><span class="ev"> — <!-- -->Addresses the &#x27;Business / identity verification&#x27; blocker the merchant is stuck on.</span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--warn)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg><span><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Right play for the engagement state</span><span class="ev"> — <!-- -->Merchant is &#x27;ghosted&#x27; but the draft is a bare step nudge with no re-engagement/value framing.</span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">No over-promising (incl. implied / typicality claims)</span><span class="ev"> — <!-- -->No unsubstantiated outcome / implied-typicality language detected.</span></span></li></ul><p class="ds-meta-line">mode: <!-- -->DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE<!-- --> · model: <!-- -->deterministic-domain-judge<!-- --> · cost: $<!-- -->0.00<!-- --> · advisory — does not change the send decision</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>6 · Eval / quality</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">An independent measurement of draft quality across four dimensions — the deep-AI showcase, in human terms.</p><div class="ds-grid g4" style="margin-top:0"><div style="border:1px solid var(--rule);border-radius:10px;padding:12px style="font-family:var(--ff-sans);font-size:12px">4<!-- -->/<!-- -->4<!-- --> dimensions passing.</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>7 · Human-in-the-loop gate</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A person decides — hold, reject, or send. Low-risk, clean drafts are eligible to send (simulated); high-risk ones are held for approval.</p><div><div class="ds-held"><div class="h"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg>Held for human approval (<!-- -->high_risk<!-- -->).</div><div class="p">No message is sent until a human approves.</div></div><div class="ds-gate-btns"><button type="button" disabled="" class="ds-btn primary">Approve &amp; send</button><button type="button" disabled="" class="ds-btn" style="color:var(--error);border-color:rgba(217, 45, 32, 0.4)">Reject</button><button type="button" disabled="" class="ds-btn">Hold</button></div><p class="ds-gate-note">Display of the gate state. Interactive approval (writing the decision back) lands in Phase C.</p></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>8 · Audit trail</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">Every step of the decision, recorded.</p><ol class="ds-audit"><li><span class="a-actor">system</span><span class="a-action">TRIAGE</span><span class="a-detail">risk=218 (High); blocker=business_verification_needed; engagement=ghosted; play=re_engagement</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">draft</span><span class="a-action">DETERMINISTIC_RULES</span><span class="a-detail">Outreach drafted by the deterministic stub (REPLAY; a recorded real-Gemini run is on the Eval page).</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">gatekeeper</span><span class="a-action">WARN</span><span class="a-detail">0 failure(s), 1 warning(s).</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">judge</span><span class="a-action">DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE</span><span class="a-detail">3/3 prose assertions supported by merchant data; all supported.</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">domain</span><span class="a-action">DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE</span><span class="a-detail">2/3 domain-quality dimensions passed → domain quality flagged (advisory — surfaced for review; does not change the send or eligibility).</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">eval</span><span class="a-action">PASS</span><span class="a-detail">4/4 quality dimensions passed.</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">system</span><span class="a-action">DRAFTED</span><span class="a-detail">Held for human approval before any send.</span></li></ol></section></div><p class="ds-note warn" style="margin-top:24px">The merchant names shown are FICTIONAL (no real businesses — so synthetic risk states are never attached to real people). The product&#x27;s adapter ingests real DataSF public-record names (PDDL 1.0, public domain — name + category only, PII-scrubbed; see lib/ingest/sf-adapter.ts). Activation state is synthetic and illustrative. No real merchant relationship or account.</p></main><!--$--><!--/$--></div><footer class="site-footer"><div class="site-footer-in"><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Demo / portfolio prototype.</span> <!-- -->REPLAY over <span style="font-weight:600">fictional display names</span> +<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">synthetic activation state</span>(the adapter ingests real public DataSF records; the demo shows invented ones) — not production logs, real sends, real marketplace access, or real-impact data. The “real Gemini” output shown is a<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">recorded static fixture</span> (reproduce it locally with your own key). diagnosis</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">How stuck this merchant is, and exactly what&#x27;s blocking them — by an auditable rule, not a model guess.</p><dl class="ds-fields"><div class="ds-field"><dt>Risk score</dt><dd>126 (High)</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Blocker</dt><dd>business_hours_needed</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Next best action</dt><dd>set_business_hours</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Days since signup</dt><dd>26</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Last login (days ago)</dt><dd>18</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Reason codes</dt><dd>inactivity, tenure_pressure</dd></div></dl><p class="ds-formula">risk = 2×<!-- -->26<!-- --> + 3×<!-- -->18<!-- --> + 10×(<!-- -->5<!-- -->−<!-- -->3<!-- -->) = <!-- -->126<span class="dim">  <!-- --># <!-- -->2<!-- --> step<!-- -->s<!-- --> remaining</span></p><div class="ds-sub"><div class="ds-tags"><span class="ds-tag role">Why they&#x27;re stuck</span><span class="ds-tag">dormant</span><span class="ds-tag">merchant side</span></div><p class="ds-card-p" style="margin-top:10px">Made progress then went inactive — likely lost momentum/motivation; re-engage before the hours &amp; availability nudge.</p><div style="margin-top:10px;border:1px solid var(--rule);background:var(--bg);border-radius:8px;padding:10px 12px"><span class="ds-tag role" style="background:none;border:none;padding:0">Recommended play ·<!-- --> </span><span style="font-size:12px;font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">re engagement</span><p class="ds-card-p" style="margin-top:6px">Re-engage acknowledging their earlier progress, THEN: One-click &#x27;set your store hours to go live&#x27; checklist nudge.</p><p class="ds-card-tech" style="font-family:var(--ff-sans)">Advanced through onboarding, then went inactive — momentum lost; re-engage before the step nudge (a step-only nudge to a quiet merchant under-performs).</p></div><p class="ds-gate-note" style="margin-top:8px">Engagement state is a snapshot inference (last_login × steps × tenure), not a measured event. Pinning the exact root cause needs instrumentation: hours_populated_flag.</p></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>2 · Drafted outreach</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A bounded, schema-constrained draft. Here it&#x27;s the deterministic stub (REPLAY); a recorded real-Gemini run is on the Eval page — the safety machinery around it is identical either way.</p><div class="ds-draft"><div class="ds-draft-sub">Your next onboarding step with Curbside Commons</div><div class="ds-draft-body">Hi Sunset Noodle House, thanks for getting started on your Curbside Commons onboarding. Your next step is to set your business hours. Reply to this message if you would like a hand.</div></div><p class="ds-card-tech" style="font-family:var(--ff-sans);color:var(--graphite)">Claims (each declared claim, verified against the stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><code class="ds-code">next_best_action<!-- --> = <!-- -->set_business_hours</code><span style="color:var(--muted)">→ merchant.<!-- -->next_best_action<!-- --> = <!-- -->set_business_hours</span></li></ul><p class="ds-meta-line">mode: <!-- -->DETERMINISTIC_RULES<!-- --> · cost: $<!-- -->0.00<!-- --> · model: <!-- -->stub-deterministic.v1</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>3 · Claims-gatekeeper</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A deterministic firewall: the draft cannot reach a human unless every declared claim checks out against the merchant&#x27;s data and no forbidden-claim pattern is present.</p><div class="ds-judge-h"><span class="ds-chip warn"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg>WARN</span><span class="ds-judge-count">Approved for the human gate</span></div><div class="ds-flaglist" style="color:var(--ink-2)"><span class="fl-l">Guardrail flags<!-- -->: </span><span style="color:var(--muted)">none — clean</span></div><div class="ds-flaglist" style="color:var(--ink-2)"><span class="fl-l">Failures<!-- -->: </span><span style="color:var(--muted)">none</span></div><div class="ds-flaglist" style="color:var(--ink-2)"><span class="fl-l">Warnings<!-- -->: </span><ul class="ds-flaglist warn"><li>held for human review (high_risk)</li></ul></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>4 · Faithfulness check (semantic judge)</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A second, independent check: an LLM from a DIFFERENT model family reads the finished message and verifies each factual sentence against the merchant&#x27;s data row — catching an invented number, capability, or timeline the deterministic gatekeeper structurally can&#x27;t see. Here it&#x27;s the deterministic stub verdict (REPLAY, $0); the live cross-family judge (Groq gpt-oss-120b) is key-gated.</p><div class="ds-judge-h"><span class="ds-chip pass"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg>ALL SUPPORTED</span><span class="ds-judge-count">3<!-- -->/<!-- -->3<!-- --> <!-- -->prose assertions backed by the data row</span></div><ul class="ds-judge-list"><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span>Your next onboarding step with Curbside Commons.<span class="ev"> <!-- -->→ <code class="ds-code">steps_completed</code></span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span>Hi Sunset Noodle House, thanks for getting started on your Curbside Commons onboarding.<span class="ev"> <!-- -->→ <code class="ds-code">merchant_name</code></span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span>Your next step is to set your business hours.<span class="ev"> <!-- -->→ <code class="ds-code">steps_completed</code></span></span></li></ul><p class="ds-meta-line">mode: <!-- -->DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE<!-- --> · model: <!-- -->deterministic-judge<!-- --> · cost: $<!-- -->0.00</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>5 · Domain quality check (domain judge)</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A third, independent check — on a different question than faithfulness. Not &#x27;is every fact true?&#x27; but &#x27;is this a GOOD activation message?&#x27; — scored against a cited rubric: matched to the merchant&#x27;s real blocker · the right play for their engagement state · no over-promising. It&#x27;s advisory and recall-favoring: the verdict is surfaced for the reviewer and recorded in the audit trail, but it never changes the send — eligibility and the human approval gate stay deterministic (a low-risk draft can still be simulated-sent even when flagged). Here BOTH the draft and this verdict are deterministic $0 stubs (REPLAY) — a minimal stub nudge often trips the engagement-fit check, which is the tertiary control doing its job, not the product grading its real output down; the live cross-family judge (Groq gpt-oss-120b) and the real drafter are separate and key-gated.</p><div class="ds-judge-h"><span class="ds-chip warn"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg>FLAGGED FOR REVIEW</span><span class="ds-judge-count">2<!-- -->/<!-- -->3<!-- --> domain-quality dimensions passed</span></div><ul class="ds-judge-list"><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Matched to the merchant&#x27;s actual blocker</span><span class="ev"> — <!-- -->Addresses the &#x27;Hours &amp; availability&#x27; blocker the merchant is stuck on.</span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--warn)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg><span><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Right play for the engagement state</span><span class="ev"> — <!-- -->Merchant is &#x27;dormant&#x27; but the draft is a bare step nudge with no re-engagement/value framing.</span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">No over-promising (incl. implied / typicality claims)</span><span class="ev"> — <!-- -->No unsubstantiated outcome / implied-typicality language detected.</span></span></li></ul><p class="ds-meta-line">mode: <!-- -->DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE<!-- --> · model: <!-- -->deterministic-domain-judge<!-- --> · cost: $<!-- -->0.00<!-- --> · advisory — does not change the send decision</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>6 · Eval / quality</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">An independent measurement of draft quality across four dimensions — the deep-AI showcase, in human terms.</p><div class="ds-grid g4" style="margin-top:0"><div style="border:1px solid var(--rule);border-radius:10px;padding:12px 14px"><div style="display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;gap:8px"><span style="font-size:13px;font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">structure</span><span class="ds-verdict ok"><svg style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A person decides — hold, reject, or send. Low-risk, clean drafts are eligible to send (simulated); high-risk ones are held for approval.</p><div><div class="ds-held"><div class="h"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 3v14 M5 3.8h8.6l-2 3 2 3H5"></path></svg>Held for human approval (<!-- -->high_risk<!-- -->).</div><div class="p">No message is sent until a human approves.</div></div><div class="ds-gate-btns"><button type="button" disabled="" class="ds-btn primary">Approve &amp; send</button><button type="button" disabled="" class="ds-btn" style="color:var(--error);border-color:rgba(217, 45, 32, 0.4)">Reject</button><button type="button" disabled="" class="ds-btn">Hold</button></div><p class="ds-gate-note">Display of the gate state. Interactive approval (writing the decision back) lands in Phase C.</p></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>8 · Audit trail</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">Every step of the decision, recorded.</p><ol class="ds-audit"><li><span class="a-actor">system</span><span class="a-action">TRIAGE</span><span class="a-detail">risk=126 (High); blocker=business_hours_needed; engagement=dormant; play=re_engagement</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">draft</span><span class="a-action">DETERMINISTIC_RULES</span><span class="a-detail">Outreach drafted by the deterministic stub (REPLAY; a recorded real-Gemini run is on the Eval page).</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">gatekeeper</span><span class="a-action">WARN</span><span class="a-detail">0 failure(s), 1 warning(s).</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">judge</span><span class="a-action">DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE</span><span class="a-detail">3/3 prose assertions supported by merchant data; all supported.</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">domain</span><span class="a-action">DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE</span><span class="a-detail">2/3 domain-quality dimensions passed → domain quality flagged (advisory — surfaced for review; does not change the send or eligibility).</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">eval</span><span class="a-action">PASS</span><span class="a-detail">4/4 quality dimensions passed.</span></li><li><span class="a-actor">system</span><span class="a-action">DRAFTED</span><span class="a-detail">Held for human approval before any send.</span></li></ol></section></div><p class="ds-note warn" style="margin-top:24px">The merchant names shown are FICTIONAL (no real businesses — so synthetic risk states are never attached to real people). The product&#x27;s adapter ingests real DataSF public-record names (PDDL 1.0, public domain — name + category only, PII-scrubbed; see lib/ingest/sf-adapter.ts). Activation state is synthetic and illustrative. No real merchant relationship or account.</p></main><!--$--><!--/$--></div><footer class="site-footer"><div class="site-footer-in"><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Demo / portfolio prototype.</span> <!-- -->REPLAY over <span style="font-weight:600">fictional display names</span> +<!-- --> <span style="font-weight:600">synthetic activation state</span>(the adapter ingests real public DataSF records; the demo shows invented ones) — not production logs, real sends, real marketplace access, or real-impact data. diagnosis</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">How stuck this merchant is, and exactly what&#x27;s blocking them — by an auditable rule, not a model guess.</p><dl class="ds-fields"><div class="ds-field"><dt>Risk score</dt><dd>55 (Medium)</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Blocker</dt><dd>final_verification_needed</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Next best action</dt><dd>complete_final_verification</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Days since signup</dt><dd>23</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Last login (days ago)</dt><dd>3</dd></div><div class="ds-field"><dt>Reason codes</dt><dd>tenure_pressure</dd></div></dl><p class="ds-formula">risk = 2×<!-- -->23<!-- --> + 3×<!-- -->3<!-- --> + 10×(<!-- -->5<!-- -->−<!-- -->5<!-- -->) = <!-- -->55<span class="dim">  <!-- --># <!-- -->0<!-- --> step<!-- -->s<!-- --> remaining</span></p><div class="ds-sub"><div class="ds-tags"><span class="ds-tag role">Why they&#x27;re stuck</span><span class="ds-tag">progressing</span><span class="ds-tag">merchant side</span></div><p class="ds-card-p" style="margin-top:10px">Engaged but blocked at final verification; the precise cause needs the final_review_status signal.</p><div style="margin-top:10px;border:1px solid var(--rule);background:var(--bg);border-radius:8px;padding:10px 12px"><span class="ds-tag role" style="background:none;border:none;padding:0">Recommended play ·<!-- --> </span><span style="font-size:12px;font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">self serve nudge</span><p class="ds-card-p" style="margin-top:6px">Nudge the final review step that flips the store live.</p><p class="ds-card-tech" style="font-family:var(--ff-sans)">Engaged and progressing — a step-matched self-serve nudge keeps momentum.</p></div><p class="ds-gate-note" style="margin-top:8px">Engagement state is a snapshot inference (last_login × steps × tenure), not a measured event. Pinning the exact root cause needs instrumentation: final_review_status.</p></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>2 · Drafted outreach</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A bounded, schema-constrained draft. Here it&#x27;s the deterministic stub (REPLAY); a recorded real-Gemini run is on the Eval page — the safety machinery around it is identical either way.</p><div class="ds-draft"><div class="ds-draft-sub">Your next onboarding step with Curbside Commons</div><div class="ds-draft-body">Hi Twin Peaks Threads, thanks for getting started on your Curbside Commons onboarding. Your next step is to complete your final verification. Reply to this message if you would like a hand.</div></div><p class="ds-card-tech" style="font-family:var(--ff-sans);color:var(--graphite)">Claims (each declared claim, verified against the merchant&#x27;s data):</p><ul class="ds-claims"><li><svg style="width:13px;height:13px;color:var(--ok);flex:none" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><code class="ds-code">steps_completed<!-- --> = <!-- -->5</code><span style="color:var(--muted)">→ merchant.<!-- -->steps_completed<!-- --> = <!-- -->5</span></li><li><svg style="width:13px;height:13px;color:var(--ok);flex:none" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><code class="ds-code">total_steps<!-- --> = <!-- -->5</code><span style="color:var(--muted)">→ merchant.<!-- -->total_steps<!-- --> = <!-- -->5</span></li><li><svg style="width:13px;height:13px;color:var(--ok);flex:none" viewBox="0 0 20 20" class="ds-code">current_blocker_code<!-- --> = <!-- -->final_verification_needed</code><span style="color:var(--muted)">→ merchant.<!-- -->current_blocker_code<!-- --> = <!-- -->final_verification_needed</span></li><li><svg style="width:13px;height:13px;color:var(--ok);flex:none" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><code class="ds-code">next_best_action<!-- --> = <!-- -->complete_final_verification</code><span style="color:var(--muted)">→ merchant.<!-- -->next_best_action<!-- --> = <!-- -->complete_final_verification</span></li></ul><p class="ds-meta-line">mode: <!-- -->DETERMINISTIC_RULES<!-- --> · cost: $<!-- -->0.00<!-- --> · model: <!-- -->stub-deterministic.v1</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>3 · Claims-gatekeeper</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A deterministic firewall: the draft cannot reach a human unless every declared claim checks out against the merchant&#x27;s data and no forbidden-claim pattern is present.</p><div class="ds-judge-h"><span class="ds-chip pass"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg>PASS</span><span class="ds-judge-count">Approved for the human gate</span></div><div class="ds-flaglist" style="color:var(--ink-2)"><span class="fl-l">Guardrail flags<!-- -->: </span><span style="color:var(--muted)">none — clean</span></div><div class="ds-flaglist" style="color:var(--ink-2)"><span class="fl-l">Failures<!-- -->: </span><span style="color:var(--muted)">none</span></div><div class="ds-flaglist" style="color:var(--ink-2)"><span class="fl-l">Warnings<!-- -->: </span><span style="color:var(--muted)">none</span></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>4 · Faithfulness check (semantic judge)</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A second, independent check: an LLM from a DIFFERENT model family reads the finished message and verifies each factual sentence against the merchant&#x27;s data row — catching an invented number, capability, or timeline the deterministic gatekeeper structurally can&#x27;t see. Here it&#x27;s the deterministic stub verdict (REPLAY, $0); the live cross-family judge (Groq gpt-oss-120b) is key-gated.</p><div class="ds-judge-h"><span class="ds-chip pass"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg>ALL SUPPORTED</span><span class="ds-judge-count">3<!-- -->/<!-- -->3<!-- --> <!-- -->prose assertions backed by the data row</span></div><ul class="ds-judge-list"><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span>Your next onboarding step with Curbside Commons.<span class="ev"> <!-- -->→ <code class="ds-code">steps_completed</code></span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span>Hi Twin Peaks Threads, thanks for getting started on your Curbside Commons onboarding.<span class="ev"> <!-- -->→ <code class="ds-code">merchant_name</code></span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span>Your next step is to complete your final verification.<span class="ev"> <!-- -->→ <code class="ds-code">steps_completed</code></span></span></li></ul><p class="ds-meta-line">mode: <!-- -->DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE<!-- --> · model: <!-- -->deterministic-judge<!-- --> · cost: $<!-- -->0.00</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>5 · Domain quality check (domain judge)</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A third, independent check — on a different question than faithfulness. Not &#x27;is every fact true?&#x27; but &#x27;is this a GOOD activation message?&#x27; — scored against a cited rubric: matched to the merchant&#x27;s real blocker · the right play for their engagement state · no over-promising. It&#x27;s advisory and recall-favoring: the verdict is surfaced for the reviewer and recorded in the audit trail, but it never changes the send — eligibility and the human approval gate stay deterministic (a low-risk draft can still be simulated-sent even when flagged). Here BOTH the draft and this verdict are deterministic $0 stubs (REPLAY) — a minimal stub nudge often trips the engagement-fit check, which is the tertiary control doing its job, not the product grading its real output down; the live cross-family judge (Groq gpt-oss-120b) and the real drafter are separate and key-gated.</p><div class="ds-judge-h"><span class="ds-chip pass"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg>GOOD PRACTICE</span><span class="ds-judge-count">3<!-- -->/<!-- -->3<!-- --> domain-quality dimensions passed</span></div><ul class="ds-judge-list"><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Matched to the merchant&#x27;s actual blocker</span><span class="ev"> — <!-- -->Addresses the &#x27;Final verification&#x27; blocker the merchant is stuck on.</span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">Right play for the engagement state</span><span class="ev"> — <!-- -->Fits the &#x27;progressing&#x27; engagement state.</span></span></li><li><svg style="color:var(--ok)" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg><span><span style="font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">No over-promising (incl. implied / typicality claims)</span><span class="ev"> — <!-- -->No unsubstantiated outcome / implied-typicality language detected.</span></span></li></ul><p class="ds-meta-line">mode: <!-- -->DETERMINISTIC_JUDGE<!-- --> · model: <!-- -->deterministic-domain-judge<!-- --> · cost: $<!-- -->0.00<!-- --> · advisory — does not change the send decision</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>6 · Eval / quality</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">An independent measurement of draft quality across four dimensions — the deep-AI showcase, in human terms.</p><div class="ds-grid g4" style="margin-top:0"><div style="border:1px solid var(--rule);border-radius:10px;padding:12px 14px"><div style="display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;gap:8px"><span style="display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;gap:8px"><span style="font-size:13px;font-weight:600;color:var(--ink)">policy</span><span class="ds-verdict ok"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg>PASS</span></div></div><div style="font-family:var(--ff-sans);font-size:12px">4<!-- -->/<!-- -->4<!-- --> dimensions passing.</p></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>7 · Human-in-the-loop gate</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">A person decides — hold, reject, or send. Low-risk, clean drafts are eligible to send (simulated); high-risk ones are held for approval.</p><div><p class="ds-verdict ok" style="font-family:var(--ff-sans);font-size:13px"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4 10.5l3.6 3.5L16 5.5"></path></svg>Eligible by the deterministic core → <span style="font-weight:600">simulated send</span> <!-- -->recorded.</p><p class="ds-meta-line">idempotency_key: <!-- -->M012:final_verification_needed:blocker_nudge.v1:2026-06-01</p></div></section><section class="ds-card flush"><h2>8 · Audit trail</h2><p class="ds-card-p" style="color:var(--muted);margin:6px 0 12px">Every step of the decision, recorded.</p><ol class="ds-audit"><li><span class="a-actor">system</span><span class="a-action">TRIAGE</span><span class="a-detail">risk=55 (Medium);
